message.content:
<command-message>plugin-dev:create-plugin</command-message>
<command-name>/plugin-dev:create-plugin</command-name>
<command-args>  hookify гҒ®д»ЈжӣҝгҒЁгҒӘгӮӢиҮӘдҪңгғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

  ## иғҢжҷҜ

  е…¬ејҸгҒ® hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ«гҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ®е•ҸйЎҢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷ:
  - гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®жӨңзҙўгҒҢ `.claude/hookify.*.local.md` гҒ®зӣёеҜҫгғ‘гӮ№гҒ®гҒҝгҒ§гҖҒCWD гҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒҷгӮӢ
  - гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ« (~/.claude/ й…ҚдёӢ) гҒ«йқһеҜҫеҝң
  - tool_matcher гҒҢе®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙгҒ®гҒҝгҒ§ regex жңӘеҜҫеҝң
  - upstream гҒ® PR https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official/pull/5 (гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«еҜҫеҝң) гҒҢй•·жңҹй–“гғһгғјгӮёгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒӘгҒ„

  ## иҰҒд»¶

  ### гғ«гғјгғ«жӨңзҙўгғ‘гӮ№ (е„Әе…Ҳй ҶдҪҚ: дҪҺ вҶ’ й«ҳ)
  1. гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/` й…ҚдёӢгҒ® `*.md` гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«
  2. гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ: `$CWD/.claude/hooks-rules/` й…ҚдёӢгҒ® `*.md` гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«
  - еҗҢеҗҚгғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеҒҙгҒҢе„Әе…Ҳ (дёҠжӣёгҒҚ)
  - гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеҒҙгҒ§ `enabled: false` гҒ«гҒҷгӮҢгҒ°гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–еҸҜиғҪ

  ### гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҪўејҸ
  hookify дә’жҸӣгҒ® YAML frontmatter + markdown гғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёеҪўејҸгӮ’з¶ӯжҢҒ:
  ```markdown
  ---
  name: rule-name
  enabled: true
  event: bash|file|stop|prompt|all
  pattern: regex-pattern
  action: warn|block
  tool_matcher: regex-pattern  # вҶҗ regex еҜҫеҝңгҒ«жӢЎејө
  conditions:
    - field: command|file_path|new_text|content|query|tool_name
      operator: regex_match|contains|equals|not_contains|starts_with|ends_with
      pattern: pattern
  ---

  гғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёжң¬ж–Ү

  hookify гҒӢгӮүгҒ®ж”№е–„зӮ№

  1. tool_matcher гӮ’ regex еҜҫеҝң: mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).* гҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒҢдҪҝгҒҲгӮӢ
  2. tool_name гӮ’ condition гҒ® field гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰиҝҪеҠ : field: tool_name гҒ§ tool еҗҚгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢжқЎд»¶гҒҢжӣёгҒ‘гӮӢ
  3. гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘз®ЎзҗҶ: hookify.*.local.md гҒ® flat з®ЎзҗҶгҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒҸ hooks-rules/
  гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ«ж•ҙзҗҶ
  4. гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ« + гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ® 2 йҡҺеұӨ: CWD гҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„зўәе®ҹгҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«иӘӯгҒҝиҫјгҒҝ

  гғ•гғғгӮҜеҜҫиұЎгӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ

  hookify гҒЁеҗҢж§ҳ:
  - PreToolUse (bash, file, all)
  - PostToolUse
  - Stop
  - UserPromptSubmit

  жҠҖиЎ“иҰҒд»¶

  - иЁҖиӘһ: Python3
  - Claude Code гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғіеҪўејҸ (plugin.json, hooks/, skills/ зӯү)
  - hookify гҒ® hooks.json ж§ӢйҖ гӮ’еҸӮиҖғгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢ
  - гӮЁгғ©гғјжҷӮгҒҜеёёгҒ« exit 0 (гғ„гғјгғ«е®ҹиЎҢгӮ’гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„)
  - YAML гғ‘гғјгӮөгғјгҒҜеӨ–йғЁдҫқеӯҳгҒӘгҒ—гҒ®з°Ўжҳ“е®ҹиЈ… (hookify гҒ® extract_frontmatter гӮ’ж”№иүҜ)

  гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғіеҗҚ

  hookify

  еҸӮиҖғ

  - hookify гӮҪгғјгӮ№: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official/tree/main/plugins/hookify
  - Claude Code hooks д»•ж§ҳ: PreToolUse гҒ® input гҒҜ { tool_name, tool_input, hook_event_name }</command-args>

content.text:
# Plugin Creation Workflow

Guide the user through creating a complete, high-quality Claude Code plugin from initial concept to tested implementation. Follow a systematic approach: understand requirements, design components, clarify details, implement following best practices, validate, and test.

## Core Principles

- **Ask clarifying questions**: Identify all ambiguities about plugin purpose, triggering, scope, and components. Ask specific, concrete questions rather than making assumptions. Wait for user answers before proceeding with implementation.
- **Load relevant skills**: Use the Skill tool to load plugin-dev skills when needed (plugin-structure, hook-development, agent-development, etc.)
- **Use specialized agents**: Leverage agent-creator, plugin-validator, and skill-reviewer agents for AI-assisted development
- **Follow best practices**: Apply patterns from plugin-dev's own implementation
- **Progressive disclosure**: Create lean skills with references/examples
- **Use TodoWrite**: Track all progress throughout all phases

**Initial request:**   hookify гҒ®д»ЈжӣҝгҒЁгҒӘгӮӢиҮӘдҪңгғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

  ## иғҢжҷҜ

  е…¬ејҸгҒ® hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ«гҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ®е•ҸйЎҢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷ:
  - гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®жӨңзҙўгҒҢ `.claude/hookify.*.local.md` гҒ®зӣёеҜҫгғ‘гӮ№гҒ®гҒҝгҒ§гҖҒCWD гҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒҷгӮӢ
  - гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ« (~/.claude/ й…ҚдёӢ) гҒ«йқһеҜҫеҝң
  - tool_matcher гҒҢе®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙгҒ®гҒҝгҒ§ regex жңӘеҜҫеҝң
  - upstream гҒ® PR https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official/pull/5 (гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«еҜҫеҝң) гҒҢй•·жңҹй–“гғһгғјгӮёгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒӘгҒ„

  ## иҰҒд»¶

  ### гғ«гғјгғ«жӨңзҙўгғ‘гӮ№ (е„Әе…Ҳй ҶдҪҚ: дҪҺ вҶ’ й«ҳ)
  1. гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/` й…ҚдёӢгҒ® `*.md` гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«
  2. гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ: `$CWD/.claude/hooks-rules/` й…ҚдёӢгҒ® `*.md` гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«
  - еҗҢеҗҚгғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеҒҙгҒҢе„Әе…Ҳ (дёҠжӣёгҒҚ)
  - гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеҒҙгҒ§ `enabled: false` гҒ«гҒҷгӮҢгҒ°гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–еҸҜиғҪ

  ### гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҪўејҸ
  hookify дә’жҸӣгҒ® YAML frontmatter + markdown гғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёеҪўејҸгӮ’з¶ӯжҢҒ:
  ```markdown
  ---
  name: rule-name
  enabled: true
  event: bash|file|stop|prompt|all
  pattern: regex-pattern
  action: warn|block
  tool_matcher: regex-pattern  # вҶҗ regex еҜҫеҝңгҒ«жӢЎејө
  conditions:
    - field: command|file_path|new_text|content|query|tool_name
      operator: regex_match|contains|equals|not_contains|starts_with|ends_with
      pattern: pattern
  ---

  гғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёжң¬ж–Ү

  hookify гҒӢгӮүгҒ®ж”№е–„зӮ№

  1. tool_matcher гӮ’ regex еҜҫеҝң: mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).* гҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒҢдҪҝгҒҲгӮӢ
  2. tool_name гӮ’ condition гҒ® field гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰиҝҪеҠ : field: tool_name гҒ§ tool еҗҚгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢжқЎд»¶гҒҢжӣёгҒ‘гӮӢ
  3. гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘз®ЎзҗҶ: hookify.*.local.md гҒ® flat з®ЎзҗҶгҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒҸ hooks-rules/
  гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ«ж•ҙзҗҶ
  4. гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ« + гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ® 2 йҡҺеұӨ: CWD гҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„зўәе®ҹгҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«иӘӯгҒҝиҫјгҒҝ

  гғ•гғғгӮҜеҜҫиұЎгӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ

  hookify гҒЁеҗҢж§ҳ:
  - PreToolUse (bash, file, all)
  - PostToolUse
  - Stop
  - UserPromptSubmit

  жҠҖиЎ“иҰҒд»¶

  - иЁҖиӘһ: Python3
  - Claude Code гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғіеҪўејҸ (plugin.json, hooks/, skills/ зӯү)
  - hookify гҒ® hooks.json ж§ӢйҖ гӮ’еҸӮиҖғгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢ
  - гӮЁгғ©гғјжҷӮгҒҜеёёгҒ« exit 0 (гғ„гғјгғ«е®ҹиЎҢгӮ’гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„)
  - YAML гғ‘гғјгӮөгғјгҒҜеӨ–йғЁдҫқеӯҳгҒӘгҒ—гҒ®з°Ўжҳ“е®ҹиЈ… (hookify гҒ® extract_frontmatter гӮ’ж”№иүҜ)

  гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғіеҗҚ

  hookify

  еҸӮиҖғ

  - hookify гӮҪгғјгӮ№: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official/tree/main/plugins/hookify
  - Claude Code hooks д»•ж§ҳ: PreToolUse гҒ® input гҒҜ { tool_name, tool_input, hook_event_name }

---

## Phase 1: Discovery

**Goal**: Understand what plugin needs to be built and what problem it solves

**Actions**:
1. Create todo list with all 7 phases
2. If plugin purpose is clear from arguments:
   - Summarize understanding
   - Identify plugin type (integration, workflow, analysis, toolkit, etc.)
3. If plugin purpose is unclear, ask user:
   - What problem does this plugin solve?
   - Who will use it and when?
   - What should it do?
   - Any similar plugins to reference?
4. Summarize understanding and confirm with user before proceeding

**Output**: Clear statement of plugin purpose and target users

---

## Phase 2: Component Planning

**Goal**: Determine what plugin components are needed

**MUST load plugin-structure skill** using Skill tool before this phase.

**Actions**:
1. Load plugin-structure skill to understand component types
2. Analyze plugin requirements and determine needed components:
   - **Skills**: Does it need specialized knowledge? (hooks API, MCP patterns, etc.)
   - **Commands**: User-initiated actions? (deploy, configure, analyze)
   - **Agents**: Autonomous tasks? (validation, generation, analysis)
   - **Hooks**: Event-driven automation? (validation, notifications)
   - **MCP**: External service integration? (databases, APIs)
   - **Settings**: User configuration? (.local.md files)
3. For each component type needed, identify:
   - How many of each type
   - What each one does
   - Rough triggering/usage patterns
4. Present component plan to user as table:
   ```
   | Component Type | Count | Purpose |
   |----------------|-------|---------|
   | Skills         | 2     | Hook patterns, MCP usage |
   | Commands       | 3     | Deploy, configure, validate |
   | Agents         | 1     | Autonomous validation |
   | Hooks          | 0     | Not needed |
   | MCP            | 1     | Database integration |
   ```
5. Get user confirmation or adjustments

**Output**: Confirmed list of components to create

---

## Phase 3: Detailed Design & Clarifying Questions

**Goal**: Specify each component in detail and resolve all ambiguities

**CRITICAL**: This is one of the most important phases. DO NOT SKIP.

**Actions**:
1. For each component in the plan, identify underspecified aspects:
   - **Skills**: What triggers them? What knowledge do they provide? How detailed?
   - **Commands**: What arguments? What tools? Interactive or automated?
   - **Agents**: When to trigger (proactive/reactive)? What tools? Output format?
   - **Hooks**: Which events? Prompt or command based? Validation criteria?
   - **MCP**: What server type? Authentication? Which tools?
   - **Settings**: What fields? Required vs optional? Defaults?

2. **Present all questions to user in organized sections** (one section per component type)

3. **Wait for answers before proceeding to implementation**

4. If user says "whatever you think is best", provide specific recommendations and get explicit confirmation

**Example questions for a skill**:
- What specific user queries should trigger this skill?
- Should it include utility scripts? What functionality?
- How detailed should the core SKILL.md be vs references/?
- Any real-world examples to include?

**Example questions for an agent**:
- Should this agent trigger proactively after certain actions, or only when explicitly requested?
- What tools does it need (Read, Write, Bash, etc.)?
- What should the output format be?
- Any specific quality standards to enforce?

**Output**: Detailed specification for each component

---

## Phase 4: Plugin Structure Creation

**Goal**: Create plugin directory structure and manifest

**Actions**:
1. Determine plugin name (kebab-case, descriptive)
2. Choose plugin location:
   - Ask user: "Where should I create the plugin?"
   - Offer options: current directory, ../new-plugin-name, custom path
3. Create directory structure using bash:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p plugin-name/.claude-plugin
   mkdir -p plugin-name/skills     # if needed
   mkdir -p plugin-name/commands   # if needed
   mkdir -p plugin-name/agents     # if needed
   mkdir -p plugin-name/hooks      # if needed
   ```
4. Create plugin.json manifest using Write tool:
   ```json
   {
     "name": "plugin-name",
     "version": "0.1.0",
     "description": "[brief description]",
     "author": {
       "name": "[author from user or default]",
       "email": "[email or default]"
     }
   }
   ```
5. Create README.md template
6. Create .gitignore if needed (for .claude/*.local.md, etc.)
7. Initialize git repo if creating new directory

**Output**: Plugin directory structure created and ready for components

---

## Phase 5: Component Implementation

**Goal**: Create each component following best practices

**LOAD RELEVANT SKILLS** before implementing each component type:
- Skills: Load skill-development skill
- Commands: Load command-development skill
- Agents: Load agent-development skill
- Hooks: Load hook-development skill
- MCP: Load mcp-integration skill
- Settings: Load plugin-settings skill

**Actions for each component**:

### For Skills:
1. Load skill-development skill using Skill tool
2. For each skill:
   - Ask user for concrete usage examples (or use from Phase 3)
   - Plan resources (scripts/, references/, examples/)
   - Create skill directory structure
   - Write SKILL.md with:
     - Third-person description with specific trigger phrases
     - Lean body (1,500-2,000 words) in imperative form
     - References to supporting files
   - Create reference files for detailed content
   - Create example files for working code
   - Create utility scripts if needed
3. Use skill-reviewer agent to validate each skill

### For Commands:
1. Load command-development skill using Skill tool
2. For each command:
   - Write command markdown with frontmatter
   - Include clear description and argument-hint
   - Specify allowed-tools (minimal necessary)
   - Write instructions FOR Claude (not TO user)
   - Provide usage examples and tips
   - Reference relevant skills if applicable

### For Agents:
1. Load agent-development skill using Skill tool
2. For each agent, use agent-creator agent:
   - Provide description of what agent should do
   - Agent-creator generates: identifier, whenToUse with examples, systemPrompt
   - Create agent markdown file with frontmatter and system prompt
   - Add appropriate model, color, and tools
   - Validate with validate-agent.sh script

### For Hooks:
1. Load hook-development skill using Skill tool
2. For each hook:
   - Create hooks/hooks.json with hook configuration
   - Prefer prompt-based hooks for complex logic
   - Use /Users/s01059/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/plugin-dev/261ce4fba4f2 for portability
   - Create hook scripts if needed (in examples/ not scripts/)
   - Test with validate-hook-schema.sh and test-hook.sh utilities

### For MCP:
1. Load mcp-integration skill using Skill tool
2. Create .mcp.json configuration with:
   - Server type (stdio for local, SSE for hosted)
   - Command and args (with /Users/s01059/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/plugin-dev/261ce4fba4f2)
   - extensionToLanguage mapping if LSP
   - Environment variables as needed
3. Document required env vars in README
4. Provide setup instructions

### For Settings:
1. Load plugin-settings skill using Skill tool
2. Create settings template in README
3. Create example .claude/plugin-name.local.md file (as documentation)
4. Implement settings reading in hooks/commands as needed
5. Add to .gitignore: `.claude/*.local.md`

**Progress tracking**: Update todos as each component is completed

**Output**: All plugin components implemented

---

## Phase 6: Validation & Quality Check

**Goal**: Ensure plugin meets quality standards and works correctly

**Actions**:
1. **Run plugin-validator agent**:
   - Use plugin-validator agent to comprehensively validate plugin
   - Check: manifest, structure, naming, components, security
   - Review validation report

2. **Fix critical issues**:
   - Address any critical errors from validation
   - Fix any warnings that indicate real problems

3. **Review with skill-reviewer** (if plugin has skills):
   - For each skill, use skill-reviewer agent
   - Check description quality, progressive disclosure, writing style
   - Apply recommendations

4. **Test agent triggering** (if plugin has agents):
   - For each agent, verify <example> blocks are clear
   - Check triggering conditions are specific
   - Run validate-agent.sh on agent files

5. **Test hook configuration** (if plugin has hooks):
   - Run validate-hook-schema.sh on hooks/hooks.json
   - Test hook scripts with test-hook.sh
   - Verify /Users/s01059/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/plugin-dev/261ce4fba4f2 usage

6. **Present findings**:
   - Summary of validation results
   - Any remaining issues
   - Overall quality assessment

7. **Ask user**: "Validation complete. Issues found: [count critical], [count warnings]. Would you like me to fix them now, or proceed to testing?"

**Output**: Plugin validated and ready for testing

---

## Phase 7: Testing & Verification

**Goal**: Test that plugin works correctly in Claude Code

**Actions**:
1. **Installation instructions**:
   - Show user how to test locally:
     ```bash
     cc --plugin-dir /path/to/plugin-name
     ```
   - Or copy to `.claude-plugin/` for project testing

2. **Verification checklist** for user to perform:
   - [ ] Skills load when triggered (ask questions with trigger phrases)
   - [ ] Commands appear in `/help` and execute correctly
   - [ ] Agents trigger on appropriate scenarios
   - [ ] Hooks activate on events (if applicable)
   - [ ] MCP servers connect (if applicable)
   - [ ] Settings files work (if applicable)

3. **Testing recommendations**:
   - For skills: Ask questions using trigger phrases from descriptions
   - For commands: Run `/plugin-name:command-name` with various arguments
   - For agents: Create scenarios matching agent examples
   - For hooks: Use `claude --debug` to see hook execution
   - For MCP: Use `/mcp` to verify servers and tools

4. **Ask user**: "I've prepared the plugin for testing. Would you like me to guide you through testing each component, or do you want to test it yourself?"

5. **If user wants guidance**, walk through testing each component with specific test cases

**Output**: Plugin tested and verified working

---

## Phase 8: Documentation & Next Steps

**Goal**: Ensure plugin is well-documented and ready for distribution

**Actions**:
1. **Verify README completeness**:
   - Check README has: overview, features, installation, prerequisites, usage
   - For MCP plugins: Document required environment variables
   - For hook plugins: Explain hook activation
   - For settings: Provide configuration templates

2. **Add marketplace entry** (if publishing):
   - Show user how to add to marketplace.json
   - Help draft marketplace description
   - Suggest category and tags

3. **Create summary**:
   - Mark all todos complete
   - List what was created:
     - Plugin name and purpose
     - Components created (X skills, Y commands, Z agents, etc.)
     - Key files and their purposes
     - Total file count and structure
   - Next steps:
     - Testing recommendations
     - Publishing to marketplace (if desired)
     - Iteration based on usage

4. **Suggest improvements** (optional):
   - Additional components that could enhance plugin
   - Integration opportunities
   - Testing strategies

**Output**: Complete, documented plugin ready for use or publication

---

## Important Notes

### Throughout All Phases

- **Use TodoWrite** to track progress at every phase
- **Load skills with Skill tool** when working on specific component types
- **Use specialized agents** (agent-creator, plugin-validator, skill-reviewer)
- **Ask for user confirmation** at key decision points
- **Follow plugin-dev's own patterns** as reference examples
- **Apply best practices**:
  - Third-person descriptions for skills
  - Imperative form in skill bodies
  - Commands written FOR Claude
  - Strong trigger phrases
  - /Users/s01059/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/plugin-dev/261ce4fba4f2 for portability
  - Progressive disclosure
  - Security-first (HTTPS, no hardcoded credentials)

### Key Decision Points (Wait for User)

1. After Phase 1: Confirm plugin purpose
2. After Phase 2: Approve component plan
3. After Phase 3: Proceed to implementation
4. After Phase 6: Fix issues or proceed
5. After Phase 7: Continue to documentation

### Skills to Load by Phase

- **Phase 2**: plugin-structure
- **Phase 5**: skill-development, command-development, agent-development, hook-development, mcp-integration, plugin-settings (as needed)
- **Phase 6**: (agents will use skills automatically)

### Quality Standards

Every component must meet these standards:
- вң… Follows plugin-dev's proven patterns
- вң… Uses correct naming conventions
- вң… Has strong trigger conditions (skills/agents)
- вң… Includes working examples
- вң… Properly documented
- вң… Validated with utilities
- вң… Tested in Claude Code

---

## Example Workflow

### User Request
"Create a plugin for managing database migrations"

### Phase 1: Discovery
- Understand: Migration management, database schema versioning
- Confirm: User wants to create, run, rollback migrations

### Phase 2: Component Planning
- Skills: 1 (migration best practices)
- Commands: 3 (create-migration, run-migrations, rollback)
- Agents: 1 (migration-validator)
- MCP: 1 (database connection)

### Phase 3: Clarifying Questions
- Which databases? (PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.)
- Migration file format? (SQL, code-based?)
- Should agent validate before applying?
- What MCP tools needed? (query, execute, schema)

### Phase 4-8: Implementation, Validation, Testing, Documentation

---

**Begin with Phase 1: Discovery**

иҰӢгҒӨгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜ deepwiki MCP гҒ§ https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official гҒ® hookify гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ гӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®еҶ…е®№гӮ’зңҒз•ҘгҒӣгҒҡгҒ«е ұе‘ҠгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

е…¬ејҸгҒ® hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ®гӮҪгғјгӮ№гӮігғјгғүгӮ’иӘҝжҹ»гҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮд»ҘдёӢгӮ’зўәиӘҚ:

1. гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ  (е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гғӘгӮ№гғҲ)
2. plugin.json гҒ®еҶ…е®№
3. hooks/hooks.json гҒ®ж§ӢйҖ 
4. Python гӮ№гӮҜгғӘгғ—гғҲ (hooks/ й…ҚдёӢ) гҒ®е…ЁеҶ…е®№
5. skills/ й…ҚдёӢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҢгҒ°гҒқгҒ®еҶ…е®№
6. commands/ й…ҚдёӢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҢгҒ°гҒқгҒ®еҶ…е®№

hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ«гӮӨгғігӮ№гғҲгғјгғ«гҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢеҸҜиғҪжҖ§гҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷ:
- ~/.claude/plugins/ й…ҚдёӢ
- гҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ й…ҚдёӢ

иҰӢгҒӨгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜ deepwiki MCP гҒ§ https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official гҒ® hookify гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ гӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®еҶ…е®№гӮ’зңҒз•ҘгҒӣгҒҡгҒ«е ұе‘ҠгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

content.content:
1вҶ’# Hookify Plugin
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Easily create custom hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors by analyzing conversation patterns or from explicit instructions.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Overview
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’The hookify plugin makes it simple to create hooks without editing complex `hooks.json` files. Instead, you create lightweight markdown configuration files that define patterns to watch for and messages to show when those patterns match.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’**Key features:**
    10вҶ’- рҹҺҜ Analyze conversations to find unwanted behaviors automatically
    11вҶ’- рҹ“қ Simple markdown configuration files with YAML frontmatter
    12вҶ’- рҹ”Қ Regex pattern matching for powerful rules
    13вҶ’- рҹҡҖ No coding required - just describe the behavior
    14вҶ’- рҹ”„ Easy enable/disable without restarting
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’## Quick Start
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’### 1. Create Your First Rule
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’```bash
    21вҶ’/hookify Warn me when I use rm -rf commands
    22вҶ’```
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’This analyzes your request and creates `.claude/hookify.warn-rm.local.md`.
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’### 2. Test It Immediately
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’**No restart needed!** Rules take effect on the very next tool use.
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’Ask Claude to run a command that should trigger the rule:
    31вҶ’```
    32вҶ’Run rm -rf /tmp/test
    33вҶ’```
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’You should see the warning message immediately!
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’## Usage
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’### Main Command: /hookify
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’**With arguments:**
    42вҶ’```
    43вҶ’/hookify Don't use console.log in TypeScript files
    44вҶ’```
    45вҶ’Creates a rule from your explicit instructions.
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’**Without arguments:**
    48вҶ’```
    49вҶ’/hookify
    50вҶ’```
    51вҶ’Analyzes recent conversation to find behaviors you've corrected or been frustrated by.
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’### Helper Commands
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’**List all rules:**
    56вҶ’```
    57вҶ’/hookify:list
    58вҶ’```
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’**Configure rules interactively:**
    61вҶ’```
    62вҶ’/hookify:configure
    63вҶ’```
    64вҶ’Enable/disable existing rules through an interactive interface.
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’**Get help:**
    67вҶ’```
    68вҶ’/hookify:help
    69вҶ’```
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’## Rule Configuration Format
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’### Simple Rule (Single Pattern)
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’`.claude/hookify.dangerous-rm.local.md`:
    76вҶ’```markdown
    77вҶ’---
    78вҶ’name: block-dangerous-rm
    79вҶ’enabled: true
    80вҶ’event: bash
    81вҶ’pattern: rm\s+-rf
    82вҶ’action: block
    83вҶ’---
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’вҡ пёҸ **Dangerous rm command detected!**
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’This command could delete important files. Please:
    88вҶ’- Verify the path is correct
    89вҶ’- Consider using a safer approach
    90вҶ’- Make sure you have backups
    91вҶ’```
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’**Action field:**
    94вҶ’- `warn`: Shows warning but allows operation (default)
    95вҶ’- `block`: Prevents operation from executing (PreToolUse) or stops session (Stop events)
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’### Advanced Rule (Multiple Conditions)
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’`.claude/hookify.sensitive-files.local.md`:
   100вҶ’```markdown
   101вҶ’---
   102вҶ’name: warn-sensitive-files
   103вҶ’enabled: true
   104вҶ’event: file
   105вҶ’action: warn
   106вҶ’conditions:
   107вҶ’  - field: file_path
   108вҶ’    operator: regex_match
   109вҶ’    pattern: \.env$|credentials|secrets
   110вҶ’  - field: new_text
   111вҶ’    operator: contains
   112вҶ’    pattern: KEY
   113вҶ’---
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’рҹ”җ **Sensitive file edit detected!**
   116вҶ’
   117вҶ’Ensure credentials are not hardcoded and file is in .gitignore.
   118вҶ’```
   119вҶ’
   120вҶ’**All conditions must match** for the rule to trigger.
   121вҶ’
   122вҶ’## Event Types
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’- **`bash`**: Triggers on Bash tool commands
   125вҶ’- **`file`**: Triggers on Edit, Write, MultiEdit tools
   126вҶ’- **`stop`**: Triggers when Claude wants to stop (for completion checks)
   127вҶ’- **`prompt`**: Triggers on user prompt submission
   128вҶ’- **`all`**: Triggers on all events
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’## Pattern Syntax
   131вҶ’
   132вҶ’Use Python regex syntax:
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’| Pattern | Matches | Example |
   135вҶ’|---------|---------|---------|
   136вҶ’| `rm\s+-rf` | rm -rf | rm -rf /tmp |
   137вҶ’| `console\.log\(` | console.log( | console.log("test") |
   138вҶ’| `(eval\|exec)\(` | eval( or exec( | eval("code") |
   139вҶ’| `\.env$` | files ending in .env | .env, .env.local |
   140вҶ’| `chmod\s+777` | chmod 777 | chmod 777 file.txt |
   141вҶ’
   142вҶ’**Tips:**
   143вҶ’- Use `\s` for whitespace
   144вҶ’- Escape special chars: `\.` for literal dot
   145вҶ’- Use `|` for OR: `(foo|bar)`
   146вҶ’- Use `.*` to match anything
   147вҶ’- Set `action: block` for dangerous operations
   148вҶ’- Set `action: warn` (or omit) for informational warnings
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’## Examples
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’### Example 1: Block Dangerous Commands
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’```markdown
   155вҶ’---
   156вҶ’name: block-destructive-ops
   157вҶ’enabled: true
   158вҶ’event: bash
   159вҶ’pattern: rm\s+-rf|dd\s+if=|mkfs|format
   160вҶ’action: block
   161вҶ’---
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’рҹӣ‘ **Destructive operation detected!**
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’This command can cause data loss. Operation blocked for safety.
   166вҶ’Please verify the exact path and use a safer approach.
   167вҶ’```
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’**This rule blocks the operation** - Claude will not be allowed to execute these commands.
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’### Example 2: Warn About Debug Code
   172вҶ’
   173вҶ’```markdown
   174вҶ’---
   175вҶ’name: warn-debug-code
   176вҶ’enabled: true
   177вҶ’event: file
   178вҶ’pattern: console\.log\(|debugger;|print\(
   179вҶ’action: warn
   180вҶ’---
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’рҹҗӣ **Debug code detected**
   183вҶ’
   184вҶ’Remember to remove debugging statements before committing.
   185вҶ’```
   186вҶ’
   187вҶ’**This rule warns but allows** - Claude sees the message but can still proceed.
   188вҶ’
   189вҶ’### Example 3: Require Tests Before Stopping
   190вҶ’
   191вҶ’```markdown
   192вҶ’---
   193вҶ’name: require-tests-run
   194вҶ’enabled: false
   195вҶ’event: stop
   196вҶ’action: block
   197вҶ’conditions:
   198вҶ’  - field: transcript
   199вҶ’    operator: not_contains
   200вҶ’    pattern: npm test|pytest|cargo test
   201вҶ’---
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’**Tests not detected in transcript!**
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’Before stopping, please run tests to verify your changes work correctly.
   206вҶ’```
   207вҶ’
   208вҶ’**This blocks Claude from stopping** if no test commands appear in the session transcript. Enable only when you want strict enforcement.
   209вҶ’
   210вҶ’## Advanced Usage
   211вҶ’
   212вҶ’### Multiple Conditions
   213вҶ’
   214вҶ’Check multiple fields simultaneously:
   215вҶ’
   216вҶ’```markdown
   217вҶ’---
   218вҶ’name: api-key-in-typescript
   219вҶ’enabled: true
   220вҶ’event: file
   221вҶ’conditions:
   222вҶ’  - field: file_path
   223вҶ’    operator: regex_match
   224вҶ’    pattern: \.tsx?$
   225вҶ’  - field: new_text
   226вҶ’    operator: regex_match
   227вҶ’    pattern: (API_KEY|SECRET|TOKEN)\s*=\s*["']
   228вҶ’---
   229вҶ’
   230вҶ’рҹ”җ **Hardcoded credential in TypeScript!**
   231вҶ’
   232вҶ’Use environment variables instead of hardcoded values.
   233вҶ’```
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’### Operators Reference
   236вҶ’
   237вҶ’- `regex_match`: Pattern must match (most common)
   238вҶ’- `contains`: String must contain pattern
   239вҶ’- `equals`: Exact string match
   240вҶ’- `not_contains`: String must NOT contain pattern
   241вҶ’- `starts_with`: String starts with pattern
   242вҶ’- `ends_with`: String ends with pattern
   243вҶ’
   244вҶ’### Field Reference
   245вҶ’
   246вҶ’**For bash events:**
   247вҶ’- `command`: The bash command string
   248вҶ’
   249вҶ’**For file events:**
   250вҶ’- `file_path`: Path to file being edited
   251вҶ’- `new_text`: New content being added (Edit, Write)
   252вҶ’- `old_text`: Old content being replaced (Edit only)
   253вҶ’- `content`: File content (Write only)
   254вҶ’
   255вҶ’**For prompt events:**
   256вҶ’- `user_prompt`: The user's submitted prompt text
   257вҶ’
   258вҶ’**For stop events:**
   259вҶ’- Use general matching on session state
   260вҶ’
   261вҶ’## Management
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’### Enable/Disable Rules
   264вҶ’
   265вҶ’**Temporarily disable:**
   266вҶ’Edit the `.local.md` file and set `enabled: false`
   267вҶ’
   268вҶ’**Re-enable:**
   269вҶ’Set `enabled: true`
   270вҶ’
   271вҶ’**Or use interactive tool:**
   272вҶ’```
   273вҶ’/hookify:configure
   274вҶ’```
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’### Delete Rules
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’Simply delete the `.local.md` file:
   279вҶ’```bash
   280вҶ’rm .claude/hookify.my-rule.local.md
   281вҶ’```
   282вҶ’
   283вҶ’### View All Rules
   284вҶ’
   285вҶ’```
   286вҶ’/hookify:list
   287вҶ’```
   288вҶ’
   289вҶ’## Installation
   290вҶ’
   291вҶ’This plugin is part of the Claude Code Marketplace. It should be auto-discovered when the marketplace is installed.
   292вҶ’
   293вҶ’**Manual testing:**
   294вҶ’```bash
   295вҶ’cc --plugin-dir /path/to/hookify
   296вҶ’```
   297вҶ’
   298вҶ’## Requirements
   299вҶ’
   300вҶ’- Python 3.7+
   301вҶ’- No external dependencies (uses stdlib only)
   302вҶ’
   303вҶ’## Troubleshooting
   304вҶ’
   305вҶ’**Rule not triggering:**
   306вҶ’1. Check rule file exists in `.claude/` directory (in project root, not plugin directory)
   307вҶ’2. Verify `enabled: true` in frontmatter
   308вҶ’3. Test regex pattern separately
   309вҶ’4. Rules should work immediately - no restart needed
   310вҶ’5. Try `/hookify:list` to see if rule is loaded
   311вҶ’
   312вҶ’**Import errors:**
   313вҶ’- Ensure Python 3 is available: `python3 --version`
   314вҶ’- Check hookify plugin is installed
   315вҶ’
   316вҶ’**Pattern not matching:**
   317вҶ’- Test regex: `python3 -c "import re; print(re.search(r'pattern', 'text'))"`
   318вҶ’- Use unquoted patterns in YAML to avoid escaping issues
   319вҶ’- Start simple, then add complexity
   320вҶ’
   321вҶ’**Hook seems slow:**
   322вҶ’- Keep patterns simple (avoid complex regex)
   323вҶ’- Use specific event types (bash, file) instead of "all"
   324вҶ’- Limit number of active rules
   325вҶ’
   326вҶ’## Contributing
   327вҶ’
   328вҶ’Found a useful rule pattern? Consider sharing example files via PR!
   329вҶ’
   330вҶ’## Future Enhancements
   331вҶ’
   332вҶ’- Severity levels (error/warning/info distinctions)
   333вҶ’- Rule templates library
   334вҶ’- Interactive pattern builder
   335вҶ’- Hook testing utilities
   336вҶ’- JSON format support (in addition to markdown)
   337вҶ’
   338вҶ’## License
   339вҶ’
   340вҶ’MIT License
   341вҶ’

1вҶ’#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2вҶ’"""PreToolUse hook executor for hookify plugin.
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’This script is called by Claude Code before any tool executes.
     5вҶ’It reads .claude/hookify.*.local.md files and evaluates rules.
     6вҶ’"""
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’import os
     9вҶ’import sys
    10вҶ’import json
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’# Add plugin root to Python path for imports
    13вҶ’PLUGIN_ROOT = os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT')
    14вҶ’if PLUGIN_ROOT and PLUGIN_ROOT not in sys.path:
    15вҶ’    sys.path.insert(0, PLUGIN_ROOT)
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’try:
    18вҶ’    from core.config_loader import load_rules
    19вҶ’    from core.rule_engine import RuleEngine
    20вҶ’except ImportError as e:
    21вҶ’    # If imports fail, allow operation and log error
    22вҶ’    error_msg = {"systemMessage": f"Hookify import error: {e}"}
    23вҶ’    print(json.dumps(error_msg), file=sys.stdout)
    24вҶ’    sys.exit(0)
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’def main():
    28вҶ’    """Main entry point for PreToolUse hook."""
    29вҶ’    try:
    30вҶ’        # Read input from stdin
    31вҶ’        input_data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’        # Determine event type for filtering
    34вҶ’        # For PreToolUse, we use tool_name to determine "bash" vs "file" event
    35вҶ’        tool_name = input_data.get('tool_name', '')
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’        event = None
    38вҶ’        if tool_name == 'Bash':

    40вҶ’        elif tool_name in ['Edit', 'Write', 'MultiEdit']:
    41вҶ’            event = 'file'
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’        # Load rules
    44вҶ’        rules = load_rules(event=event)
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’        # Evaluate rules
    47вҶ’        engine = RuleEngine()
    48вҶ’        result = engine.evaluate_rules(rules, input_data)
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’        # Always output JSON (even if empty)
    51вҶ’        print(json.dumps(result), file=sys.stdout)
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’    except Exception as e:
    54вҶ’        # On any error, allow the operation and log
    55вҶ’        error_output = {
    56вҶ’            "systemMessage": f"Hookify error: {str(e)}"
    57вҶ’        }
    58вҶ’        print(json.dumps(error_output), file=sys.stdout)
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’    finally:
    61вҶ’        # ALWAYS exit 0 - never block operations due to hook errors
    62вҶ’        sys.exit(0)
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’if __name__ == '__main__':
    66вҶ’    main()
    67вҶ’

1вҶ’#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2вҶ’"""PostToolUse hook executor for hookify plugin.
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’This script is called by Claude Code after a tool executes.
     5вҶ’It reads .claude/hookify.*.local.md files and evaluates rules.
     6вҶ’"""
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’import os
     9вҶ’import sys
    10вҶ’import json
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’# Add plugin root to Python path for imports
    13вҶ’PLUGIN_ROOT = os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT')
    14вҶ’if PLUGIN_ROOT and PLUGIN_ROOT not in sys.path:
    15вҶ’    sys.path.insert(0, PLUGIN_ROOT)
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’try:
    18вҶ’    from core.config_loader import load_rules
    19вҶ’    from core.rule_engine import RuleEngine
    20вҶ’except ImportError as e:
    21вҶ’    error_msg = {"systemMessage": f"Hookify import error: {e}"}
    22вҶ’    print(json.dumps(error_msg), file=sys.stdout)
    23вҶ’    sys.exit(0)
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’def main():
    27вҶ’    """Main entry point for PostToolUse hook."""
    28вҶ’    try:
    29вҶ’        # Read input from stdin
    30вҶ’        input_data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’        # Determine event type based on tool
    33вҶ’        tool_name = input_data.get('tool_name', '')
    34вҶ’        event = None
    35вҶ’        if tool_name == 'Bash':
    36вҶ’            event = 'bash'
    37вҶ’        elif tool_name in ['Edit', 'Write', 'MultiEdit']:
    38вҶ’            event = 'file'
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’        # Load rules
    41вҶ’        rules = load_rules(event=event)
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’        # Evaluate rules
    44вҶ’        engine = RuleEngine()
    45вҶ’        result = engine.evaluate_rules(rules, input_data)
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’        # Always output JSON (even if empty)
    48вҶ’        print(json.dumps(result), file=sys.stdout)
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’    except Exception as e:
    51вҶ’        error_output = {
    52вҶ’            "systemMessage": f"Hookify error: {str(e)}"
    53вҶ’        }
    54вҶ’        print(json.dumps(error_output), file=sys.stdout)
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’    finally:
    57вҶ’        # ALWAYS exit 0
    58вҶ’        sys.exit(0)
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’if __name__ == '__main__':
    62вҶ’    main()
    63вҶ’

1вҶ’#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2вҶ’"""Stop hook executor for hookify plugin.
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’This script is called by Claude Code when agent wants to stop.
     5вҶ’It reads .claude/hookify.*.local.md files and evaluates stop rules.
     6вҶ’"""
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’import os
     9вҶ’import sys
    10вҶ’import json
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’# Add plugin root to Python path for imports
    13вҶ’PLUGIN_ROOT = os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT')
    14вҶ’if PLUGIN_ROOT and PLUGIN_ROOT not in sys.path:
    15вҶ’    sys.path.insert(0, PLUGIN_ROOT)
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’try:
    18вҶ’    from core.config_loader import load_rules
    19вҶ’    from core.rule_engine import RuleEngine
    20вҶ’except ImportError as e:
    21вҶ’    error_msg = {"systemMessage": f"Hookify import error: {e}"}
    22вҶ’    print(json.dumps(error_msg), file=sys.stdout)
    23вҶ’    sys.exit(0)
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’def main():
    27вҶ’    """Main entry point for Stop hook."""
    28вҶ’    try:
    29вҶ’        # Read input from stdin
    30вҶ’        input_data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’        # Load stop rules
    33вҶ’        rules = load_rules(event='stop')
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’        # Evaluate rules
    36вҶ’        engine = RuleEngine()
    37вҶ’        result = engine.evaluate_rules(rules, input_data)
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’        # Always output JSON (even if empty)
    40вҶ’        print(json.dumps(result), file=sys.stdout)
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’    except Exception as e:
    43вҶ’        # On any error, allow the operation
    44вҶ’        error_output = {
    45вҶ’            "systemMessage": f"Hookify error: {str(e)}"
    46вҶ’        }
    47вҶ’        print(json.dumps(error_output), file=sys.stdout)
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’    finally:
    50вҶ’        # ALWAYS exit 0
    51вҶ’        sys.exit(0)
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’if __name__ == '__main__':
    55вҶ’    main()
    56вҶ’

    28вҶ’    try:
    29вҶ’        # Read input from stdin
    30вҶ’        input_data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’        # Load user prompt rules
    33вҶ’        rules = load_rules(event='prompt')
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’        # Evaluate rules
    36вҶ’        engine = RuleEngine()
    37вҶ’        result = engine.evaluate_rules(rules, input_data)
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’        # Always output JSON (even if empty)
    40вҶ’        print(json.dumps(result), file=sys.stdout)
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’    except Exception as e:
    43вҶ’        error_output = {
    44вҶ’            "systemMessage": f"Hookify error: {str(e)}"
    45вҶ’        }
    46вҶ’        print(json.dumps(error_output), file=sys.stdout)
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’    finally:
    49вҶ’        # ALWAYS exit 0
    50вҶ’        sys.exit(0)
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’if __name__ == '__main__':
    54вҶ’    main()
    55вҶ’

content.content:
1вҶ’#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2вҶ’"""Configuration loader for hookify plugin.
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’Loads and parses .claude/hookify.*.local.md files.
     5вҶ’"""
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’import os
     8вҶ’import sys
     9вҶ’import glob
    10вҶ’import re
    11вҶ’from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any
    12вҶ’from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’@dataclass
    16вҶ’class Condition:
    17вҶ’    """A single condition for matching."""
    18вҶ’    field: str  # "command", "new_text", "old_text", "file_path", etc.
    19вҶ’    operator: str  # "regex_match", "contains", "equals", etc.
    20вҶ’    pattern: str  # Pattern to match
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’    @classmethod
    23вҶ’    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> 'Condition':
    24вҶ’        """Create Condition from dict."""
    25вҶ’        return cls(
    26вҶ’            field=data.get('field', ''),
    27вҶ’            operator=data.get('operator', 'regex_match'),
    28вҶ’            pattern=data.get('pattern', '')
    29вҶ’        )
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’@dataclass
    33вҶ’class Rule:
    34вҶ’    """A hookify rule."""
    35вҶ’    name: str
    36вҶ’    enabled: bool
    37вҶ’    event: str  # "bash", "file", "stop", "all", etc.
    38вҶ’    pattern: Optional[str] = None  # Simple pattern (legacy)
    39вҶ’    conditions: List[Condition] = field(default_factory=list)
    40вҶ’    action: str = "warn"  # "warn" or "block" (future)
    41вҶ’    tool_matcher: Optional[str] = None  # Override tool matching
    42вҶ’    message: str = ""  # Message body from markdown
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’    @classmethod
    45вҶ’    def from_dict(cls, frontmatter: Dict[str, Any], message: str) -> 'Rule':
    46вҶ’        """Create Rule from frontmatter dict and message body."""
    47вҶ’        # Handle both simple pattern and complex conditions
    48вҶ’        conditions = []
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’        # New style: explicit conditions list
    51вҶ’        if 'conditions' in frontmatter:
    52вҶ’            cond_list = frontmatter['conditions']
    53вҶ’            if isinstance(cond_list, list):
    54вҶ’                conditions = [Condition.from_dict(c) for c in cond_list]
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’        # Legacy style: simple pattern field
    57вҶ’        simple_pattern = frontmatter.get('pattern')
    58вҶ’        if simple_pattern and not conditions:
    59вҶ’            # Convert simple pattern to condition
    60вҶ’            # Infer field from event
    61вҶ’            event = frontmatter.get('event', 'all')
    62вҶ’            if event == 'bash':
    63вҶ’                field = 'command'
    64вҶ’            elif event == 'file':
    65вҶ’                field = 'new_text'
    66вҶ’            else:
    67вҶ’                field = 'content'
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’            conditions = [Condition(
    70вҶ’                field=field,
    71вҶ’                operator='regex_match',
    72вҶ’                pattern=simple_pattern
    73вҶ’            )]
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’        return cls(
    76вҶ’            name=frontmatter.get('name', 'unnamed'),
    77вҶ’            enabled=frontmatter.get('enabled', True),
    78вҶ’            event=frontmatter.get('event', 'all'),
    79вҶ’            pattern=simple_pattern,
    80вҶ’            conditions=conditions,
    81вҶ’            action=frontmatter.get('action', 'warn'),
    82вҶ’            tool_matcher=frontmatter.get('tool_matcher'),
    83вҶ’            message=message.strip()
    84вҶ’        )
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’def extract_frontmatter(content: str) -> tuple[Dict[str, Any], str]:
    88вҶ’    """Extract YAML frontmatter and message body from markdown.
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’    Returns (frontmatter_dict, message_body).
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’    Supports multi-line dictionary items in lists by preserving indentation.
    93вҶ’    """
    94вҶ’    if not content.startswith('---'):
    95вҶ’        return {}, content
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’    # Split on --- markers
    98вҶ’    parts = content.split('---', 2)
    99вҶ’    if len(parts) < 3:
   100вҶ’        return {}, content
   101вҶ’
   102вҶ’    frontmatter_text = parts[1]
   103вҶ’    message = parts[2].strip()
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’    # Simple YAML parser that handles indented list items
   106вҶ’    frontmatter = {}
   107вҶ’    lines = frontmatter_text.split('\n')
   108вҶ’
   109вҶ’    current_key = None
   110вҶ’    current_list = []
   111вҶ’    current_dict = {}
   112вҶ’    in_list = False
   113вҶ’    in_dict_item = False
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’    for line in lines:
   116вҶ’        # Skip empty lines and comments
   117вҶ’        stripped = line.strip()
   118вҶ’        if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   119вҶ’            continue
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’        # Check indentation level
   122вҶ’        indent = len(line) - len(line.lstrip())
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’        # Top-level key (no indentation or minimal)
   125вҶ’        if indent == 0 and ':' in line and not line.strip().startswith('-'):
   126вҶ’            # Save previous list/dict if any
   127вҶ’            if in_list and current_key:
   128вҶ’                if in_dict_item and current_dict:
   129вҶ’                    current_list.append(current_dict)
   130вҶ’                    current_dict = {}
   131вҶ’                frontmatter[current_key] = current_list
   132вҶ’                in_list = False
   133вҶ’                in_dict_item = False
   134вҶ’                current_list = []
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’            key, value = line.split(':', 1)
   137вҶ’            key = key.strip()
   138вҶ’            value = value.strip()
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’            if not value:
   141вҶ’                # Empty value - list or nested structure follows
   142вҶ’                current_key = key
   143вҶ’                in_list = True
   144вҶ’                current_list = []
   145вҶ’            else:
   146вҶ’                # Simple key-value pair
   147вҶ’                value = value.strip('"').strip("'")
   148вҶ’                if value.lower() == 'true':
   149вҶ’                    value = True
   150вҶ’                elif value.lower() == 'false':
   151вҶ’                    value = False
   152вҶ’                frontmatter[key] = value
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’        # List item (starts with -)
   155вҶ’        elif stripped.startswith('-') and in_list:
   156вҶ’            # Save previous dict item if any
   157вҶ’            if in_dict_item and current_dict:
   158вҶ’                current_list.append(current_dict)
   159вҶ’                current_dict = {}
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’            item_text = stripped[1:].strip()
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’            # Check if this is an inline dict (key: value on same line)
   164вҶ’            if ':' in item_text and ',' in item_text:
   165вҶ’                # Inline comma-separated dict: "- field: command, operator: regex_match"
   166вҶ’                item_dict = {}
   167вҶ’                for part in item_text.split(','):
   168вҶ’                    if ':' in part:
   169вҶ’                        k, v = part.split(':', 1)
   170вҶ’                        item_dict[k.strip()] = v.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
   171вҶ’                current_list.append(item_dict)
   172вҶ’                in_dict_item = False
   173вҶ’            elif ':' in item_text:
   174вҶ’                # Start of multi-line dict item: "- field: command"
   175вҶ’                in_dict_item = True
   176вҶ’                k, v = item_text.split(':', 1)
   177вҶ’                current_dict = {k.strip(): v.strip().strip('"').strip("'")}
   178вҶ’            else:
   179вҶ’                # Simple list item
   180вҶ’                current_list.append(item_text.strip('"').strip("'"))
   181вҶ’                in_dict_item = False
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’        # Continuation of dict item (indented under list item)
   184вҶ’        elif indent > 2 and in_dict_item and ':' in line:
   185вҶ’            # This is a field of the current dict item
   186вҶ’            k, v = stripped.split(':', 1)
   187вҶ’            current_dict[k.strip()] = v.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
   188вҶ’
   189вҶ’    # Save final list/dict if any
   190вҶ’    if in_list and current_key:
   191вҶ’        if in_dict_item and current_dict:
   192вҶ’            current_list.append(current_dict)
   193вҶ’        frontmatter[current_key] = current_list
   194вҶ’
   195вҶ’    return frontmatter, message
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’
   198вҶ’def load_rules(event: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Rule]:
   199вҶ’    """Load all hookify rules from .claude directory.
   200вҶ’
   201вҶ’    Args:
   202вҶ’        event: Optional event filter ("bash", "file", "stop", etc.)
   203вҶ’
   204вҶ’    Returns:
   205вҶ’        List of enabled Rule objects matching the event.
   206вҶ’    """
   207вҶ’    rules = []
   208вҶ’
   209вҶ’    # Find all hookify.*.local.md files
   210вҶ’    pattern = os.path.join('.claude', 'hookify.*.local.md')
   211вҶ’    files = glob.glob(pattern)
   212вҶ’
   213вҶ’    for file_path in files:
   214вҶ’        try:
   215вҶ’            rule = load_rule_file(file_path)
   216вҶ’            if not rule:
   217вҶ’                continue
   218вҶ’
   219вҶ’            # Filter by event if specified
   220вҶ’            if event:
   221вҶ’                if rule.event != 'all' and rule.event != event:
   222вҶ’                    continue
   223вҶ’
   224вҶ’            # Only include enabled rules
   225вҶ’            if rule.enabled:
   226вҶ’                rules.append(rule)
   227вҶ’
   228вҶ’        except (IOError, OSError, PermissionError) as e:
   229вҶ’            # File I/O errors - log and continue
   230вҶ’            print(f"Warning: Failed to read {file_path}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   231вҶ’            continue
   232вҶ’        except (ValueError, KeyError, AttributeError, TypeError) as e:
   233вҶ’            # Parsing errors - log and continue
   234вҶ’            print(f"Warning: Failed to parse {file_path}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   235вҶ’            continue
   236вҶ’        except Exception as e:
   237вҶ’            # Unexpected errors - log with type details
   238вҶ’            print(f"Warning: Unexpected error loading {file_path} ({type(e).__name__}): {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   239вҶ’            continue
   240вҶ’
   241вҶ’    return rules
   242вҶ’
   243вҶ’
   244вҶ’def load_rule_file(file_path: str) -> Optional[Rule]:
   245вҶ’    """Load a single rule file.
   246вҶ’
   247вҶ’    Returns:
   248вҶ’        Rule object or None if file is invalid.
   249вҶ’    """
   250вҶ’    try:
   251вҶ’        with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
   252вҶ’            content = f.read()
   253вҶ’
   254вҶ’        frontmatter, message = extract_frontmatter(content)
   255вҶ’
   256вҶ’        if not frontmatter:
   257вҶ’            print(f"Warning: {file_path} missing YAML frontmatter (must start with ---)", file=sys.stderr)
   258вҶ’            return None
   259вҶ’
   260вҶ’        rule = Rule.from_dict(frontmatter, message)
   261вҶ’        return rule
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’    except (IOError, OSError, PermissionError) as e:
   264вҶ’        print(f"Error: Cannot read {file_path}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   265вҶ’        return None
   266вҶ’    except (ValueError, KeyError, AttributeError, TypeError) as e:
   267вҶ’        print(f"Error: Malformed rule file {file_path}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   268вҶ’        return None
   269вҶ’    except UnicodeDecodeError as e:
   270вҶ’        print(f"Error: Invalid encoding in {file_path}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   271вҶ’        return None
   272вҶ’    except Exception as e:
   273вҶ’        print(f"Error: Unexpected error parsing {file_path} ({type(e).__name__}): {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   274вҶ’        return None
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’
   277вҶ’# For testing
   278вҶ’if __name__ == '__main__':
   279вҶ’    import sys
   280вҶ’
   281вҶ’    # Test frontmatter parsing
   282вҶ’    test_content = """---
   283вҶ’name: test-rule
   284вҶ’enabled: true
   285вҶ’event: bash
   286вҶ’pattern: "rm -rf"
   287вҶ’---
   288вҶ’
   289вҶ’вҡ пёҸ Dangerous command detected!
   290вҶ’"""
   291вҶ’
   292вҶ’    fm, msg = extract_frontmatter(test_content)
   293вҶ’    print("Frontmatter:", fm)
   294вҶ’    print("Message:", msg)
   295вҶ’
   296вҶ’    rule = Rule.from_dict(fm, msg)
   297вҶ’    print("Rule:", rule)
   298вҶ’

1. гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ  (е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гғӘгӮ№гғҲ)
2. plugin.json гҒ®еҶ…е®№

5. skills/ й…ҚдёӢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҢгҒ°гҒқгҒ®еҶ…е®№
6. commands/ й…ҚдёӢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҢгҒ°гҒқгҒ®еҶ…е®№

hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ«гӮӨгғігӮ№гғҲгғјгғ«гҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢеҸҜиғҪжҖ§гҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷ:
- ~/.claude/plugins/ й…ҚдёӢ
- гҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ й…ҚдёӢ

иҰӢгҒӨгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜ deepwiki MCP гҒ§ https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official гҒ® hookify гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ гӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®еҶ…е®№гӮ’зңҒз•ҘгҒӣгҒҡгҒ«е ұе‘ҠгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

content.content:
1вҶ’#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2вҶ’"""Rule evaluation engine for hookify plugin."""
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’import re
     5вҶ’import sys
     6вҶ’from functools import lru_cache
     7вҶ’from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’# Import from local module
    10вҶ’from core.config_loader import Rule, Condition
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’# Cache compiled regexes (max 128 patterns)
    14вҶ’@lru_cache(maxsize=128)
    15вҶ’def compile_regex(pattern: str) -> re.Pattern:
    16вҶ’    """Compile regex pattern with caching.
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’    Args:
    19вҶ’        pattern: Regex pattern string
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’    Returns:
    22вҶ’        Compiled regex pattern
    23вҶ’    """
    24вҶ’    return re.compile(pattern, re.IGNORECASE)
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’class RuleEngine:
    28вҶ’    """Evaluates rules against hook input data."""
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’    def __init__(self):
    31вҶ’        """Initialize rule engine."""
    32вҶ’        # No need for instance cache anymore - using global lru_cache
    33вҶ’        pass
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’    def evaluate_rules(self, rules: List[Rule], input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    36вҶ’        """Evaluate all rules and return combined results.
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’        Checks all rules and accumulates matches. Blocking rules take priority
    39вҶ’        over warning rules. All matching rule messages are combined.
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’        Args:
    42вҶ’            rules: List of Rule objects to evaluate
    43вҶ’            input_data: Hook input JSON (tool_name, tool_input, etc.)
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’        Returns:
    46вҶ’            Response dict with systemMessage, hookSpecificOutput, etc.
    47вҶ’            Empty dict {} if no rules match.
    48вҶ’        """
    49вҶ’        hook_event = input_data.get('hook_event_name', '')
    50вҶ’        blocking_rules = []
    51вҶ’        warning_rules = []
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’        for rule in rules:
    54вҶ’            if self._rule_matches(rule, input_data):
    55вҶ’                if rule.action == 'block':
    56вҶ’                    blocking_rules.append(rule)
    57вҶ’                else:
    58вҶ’                    warning_rules.append(rule)
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’        # If any blocking rules matched, block the operation
    61вҶ’        if blocking_rules:
    62вҶ’            messages = [f"**[{r.name}]**\n{r.message}" for r in blocking_rules]
    63вҶ’            combined_message = "\n\n".join(messages)
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’            # Use appropriate blocking format based on event type
    66вҶ’            if hook_event == 'Stop':
    67вҶ’                return {
    68вҶ’                    "decision": "block",
    69вҶ’                    "reason": combined_message,
    70вҶ’                    "systemMessage": combined_message
    71вҶ’                }
    72вҶ’            elif hook_event in ['PreToolUse', 'PostToolUse']:
    73вҶ’                return {
    74вҶ’                    "hookSpecificOutput": {
    75вҶ’                        "hookEventName": hook_event,
    76вҶ’                        "permissionDecision": "deny"
    77вҶ’                    },
    78вҶ’                    "systemMessage": combined_message
    79вҶ’                }
    80вҶ’            else:
    81вҶ’                # For other events, just show message
    82вҶ’                return {
    83вҶ’                    "systemMessage": combined_message
    84вҶ’                }
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’        # If only warnings, show them but allow operation
    87вҶ’        if warning_rules:
    88вҶ’            messages = [f"**[{r.name}]**\n{r.message}" for r in warning_rules]
    89вҶ’            return {
    90вҶ’                "systemMessage": "\n\n".join(messages)
    91вҶ’            }
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’        # No matches - allow operation
    94вҶ’        return {}
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’    def _rule_matches(self, rule: Rule, input_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    97вҶ’        """Check if rule matches input data.
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’        Args:
   100вҶ’            rule: Rule to evaluate
   101вҶ’            input_data: Hook input data
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’        Returns:
   104вҶ’            True if rule matches, False otherwise
   105вҶ’        """
   106вҶ’        # Extract tool information
   107вҶ’        tool_name = input_data.get('tool_name', '')
   108вҶ’        tool_input = input_data.get('tool_input', {})
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’        # Check tool matcher if specified
   111вҶ’        if rule.tool_matcher:
   112вҶ’            if not self._matches_tool(rule.tool_matcher, tool_name):
   113вҶ’                return False
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’        # If no conditions, don't match
   116вҶ’        # (Rules must have at least one condition to be valid)
   117вҶ’        if not rule.conditions:
   118вҶ’            return False
   119вҶ’
   120вҶ’        # All conditions must match
   121вҶ’        for condition in rule.conditions:
   122вҶ’            if not self._check_condition(condition, tool_name, tool_input, input_data):
   123вҶ’                return False
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’        return True
   126вҶ’
   127вҶ’    def _matches_tool(self, matcher: str, tool_name: str) -> bool:
   128вҶ’        """Check if tool_name matches the matcher pattern.
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’        Args:
   131вҶ’            matcher: Pattern like "Bash", "Edit|Write", "*"
   132вҶ’            tool_name: Actual tool name
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’        Returns:
   135вҶ’            True if matches
   136вҶ’        """
   137вҶ’        if matcher == '*':
   138вҶ’            return True
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’        # Split on | for OR matching
   141вҶ’        patterns = matcher.split('|')
   142вҶ’        return tool_name in patterns
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’    def _check_condition(self, condition: Condition, tool_name: str,
   145вҶ’                        tool_input: Dict[str, Any], input_data: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> bool:
   146вҶ’        """Check if a single condition matches.
   147вҶ’
   148вҶ’        Args:
   149вҶ’            condition: Condition to check
   150вҶ’            tool_name: Tool being used
   151вҶ’            tool_input: Tool input dict
   152вҶ’            input_data: Full hook input data (for Stop events, etc.)
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’        Returns:
   155вҶ’            True if condition matches
   156вҶ’        """
   157вҶ’        # Extract the field value to check
   158вҶ’        field_value = self._extract_field(condition.field, tool_name, tool_input, input_data)
   159вҶ’        if field_value is None:
   160вҶ’            return False
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’        # Apply operator
   163вҶ’        operator = condition.operator
   164вҶ’        pattern = condition.pattern
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’        if operator == 'regex_match':
   167вҶ’            return self._regex_match(pattern, field_value)
   168вҶ’        elif operator == 'contains':
   169вҶ’            return pattern in field_value
   170вҶ’        elif operator == 'equals':
   171вҶ’            return pattern == field_value
   172вҶ’        elif operator == 'not_contains':
   173вҶ’            return pattern not in field_value
   174вҶ’        elif operator == 'starts_with':
   175вҶ’            return field_value.startswith(pattern)
   176вҶ’        elif operator == 'ends_with':
   177вҶ’            return field_value.endswith(pattern)
   178вҶ’        else:
   179вҶ’            # Unknown operator
   180вҶ’            return False
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’    def _extract_field(self, field: str, tool_name: str,
   183вҶ’                      tool_input: Dict[str, Any], input_data: Dict[str, Any] = None) -> Optional[str]:
   184вҶ’        """Extract field value from tool input or hook input data.
   185вҶ’
   186вҶ’        Args:
   187вҶ’            field: Field name like "command", "new_text", "file_path", "reason", "transcript"
   188вҶ’            tool_name: Tool being used (may be empty for Stop events)
   189вҶ’            tool_input: Tool input dict
   190вҶ’            input_data: Full hook input (for accessing transcript_path, reason, etc.)
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’        Returns:
   193вҶ’            Field value as string, or None if not found
   194вҶ’        """
   195вҶ’        # Direct tool_input fields
   196вҶ’        if field in tool_input:
   197вҶ’            value = tool_input[field]
   198вҶ’            if isinstance(value, str):
   199вҶ’                return value
   200вҶ’            return str(value)
   201вҶ’
   202вҶ’        # For Stop events and other non-tool events, check input_data
   203вҶ’        if input_data:
   204вҶ’            # Stop event specific fields
   205вҶ’            if field == 'reason':
   206вҶ’                return input_data.get('reason', '')
   207вҶ’            elif field == 'transcript':
   208вҶ’                # Read transcript file if path provided
   209вҶ’                transcript_path = input_data.get('transcript_path')
   210вҶ’                if transcript_path:
   211вҶ’                    try:
   212вҶ’                        with open(transcript_path, 'r') as f:
   213вҶ’                            return f.read()
   214вҶ’                    except FileNotFoundError:
   215вҶ’                        print(f"Warning: Transcript file not found: {transcript_path}", file=sys.stderr)
   216вҶ’                        return ''
   217вҶ’                    except PermissionError:
   218вҶ’                        print(f"Warning: Permission denied reading transcript: {transcript_path}", file=sys.stderr)
   219вҶ’                        return ''
   220вҶ’                    except (IOError, OSError) as e:
   221вҶ’                        print(f"Warning: Error reading transcript {transcript_path}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   222вҶ’                        return ''
   223вҶ’                    except UnicodeDecodeError as e:
   224вҶ’                        print(f"Warning: Encoding error in transcript {transcript_path}: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   225вҶ’                        return ''
   226вҶ’            elif field == 'user_prompt':
   227вҶ’                # For UserPromptSubmit events
   228вҶ’                return input_data.get('user_prompt', '')
   229вҶ’
   230вҶ’        # Handle special cases by tool type
   231вҶ’        if tool_name == 'Bash':
   232вҶ’            if field == 'command':
   233вҶ’                return tool_input.get('command', '')
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’        elif tool_name in ['Write', 'Edit']:
   236вҶ’            if field == 'content':
   237вҶ’                # Write uses 'content', Edit has 'new_string'
   238вҶ’                return tool_input.get('content') or tool_input.get('new_string', '')
   239вҶ’            elif field == 'new_text' or field == 'new_string':
   240вҶ’                return tool_input.get('new_string', '')
   241вҶ’            elif field == 'old_text' or field == 'old_string':
   242вҶ’                return tool_input.get('old_string', '')
   243вҶ’            elif field == 'file_path':
   244вҶ’                return tool_input.get('file_path', '')
   245вҶ’
   246вҶ’        elif tool_name == 'MultiEdit':
   247вҶ’            if field == 'file_path':
   248вҶ’                return tool_input.get('file_path', '')
   249вҶ’            elif field in ['new_text', 'content']:
   250вҶ’                # Concatenate all edits
   251вҶ’                edits = tool_input.get('edits', [])
   252вҶ’                return ' '.join(e.get('new_string', '') for e in edits)
   253вҶ’
   254вҶ’        return None
   255вҶ’
   256вҶ’    def _regex_match(self, pattern: str, text: str) -> bool:
   257вҶ’        """Check if pattern matches text using regex.
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’        Args:
   260вҶ’            pattern: Regex pattern
   261вҶ’            text: Text to match against
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’        Returns:
   264вҶ’            True if pattern matches
   265вҶ’        """
   266вҶ’        try:
   267вҶ’            # Use cached compiled regex (LRU cache with max 128 patterns)
   268вҶ’            regex = compile_regex(pattern)
   269вҶ’            return bool(regex.search(text))
   270вҶ’
   271вҶ’        except re.error as e:
   272вҶ’            print(f"Invalid regex pattern '{pattern}': {e}", file=sys.stderr)
   273вҶ’            return False
   274вҶ’
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’# For testing
   277вҶ’if __name__ == '__main__':
   278вҶ’    from core.config_loader import Condition, Rule
   279вҶ’
   280вҶ’    # Test rule evaluation
   281вҶ’    rule = Rule(
   282вҶ’        name="test-rm",
   283вҶ’        enabled=True,
   284вҶ’        event="bash",
   285вҶ’        conditions=[
   286вҶ’            Condition(field="command", operator="regex_match", pattern=r"rm\s+-rf")
   287вҶ’        ],
   288вҶ’        message="Dangerous rm command!"
   289вҶ’    )
   290вҶ’
   291вҶ’    engine = RuleEngine()
   292вҶ’
   293вҶ’    # Test matching input
   294вҶ’    test_input = {
   295вҶ’        "tool_name": "Bash",
   296вҶ’        "tool_input": {
   297вҶ’            "command": "rm -rf /tmp/test"
   298вҶ’        }
   299вҶ’    }
   300вҶ’
   301вҶ’    result = engine.evaluate_rules([rule], test_input)
   302вҶ’    print("Match result:", result)
   303вҶ’
   304вҶ’    # Test non-matching input
   305вҶ’    test_input2 = {
   306вҶ’        "tool_name": "Bash",
   307вҶ’        "tool_input": {
   308вҶ’            "command": "ls -la"
   309вҶ’        }
   310вҶ’    }
   311вҶ’
   312вҶ’    result2 = engine.evaluate_rules([rule], test_input2)
   313вҶ’    print("Non-match result:", result2)
   314вҶ’

hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ«гӮӨгғігӮ№гғҲгғјгғ«гҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢеҸҜиғҪжҖ§гҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷ:

иҰӢгҒӨгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜ deepwiki MCP гҒ§ https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official гҒ® hookify гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ гӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®еҶ…е®№гӮ’зңҒз•ҘгҒӣгҒҡгҒ«е ұе‘ҠгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

е…¬ејҸгҒ® hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ®гӮҪгғјгӮ№гӮігғјгғүгӮ’иӘҝжҹ»гҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮд»ҘдёӢгӮ’зўәиӘҚ:

1. гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ  (е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гғӘгӮ№гғҲ)
2. plugin.json гҒ®еҶ…е®№
3. hooks/hooks.json гҒ®ж§ӢйҖ 
4. Python гӮ№гӮҜгғӘгғ—гғҲ (hooks/ й…ҚдёӢ) гҒ®е…ЁеҶ…е®№
5. skills/ й…ҚдёӢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҢгҒ°гҒқгҒ®еҶ…е®№
6. commands/ й…ҚдёӢгҒҢгҒӮгӮҢгҒ°гҒқгҒ®еҶ…е®№

hookify гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ«гӮӨгғігӮ№гғҲгғјгғ«гҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢеҸҜиғҪжҖ§гҒҢгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒҷ:
- ~/.claude/plugins/ й…ҚдёӢ
- гҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ ~/.claude/plugins/cache/ й…ҚдёӢ

иҰӢгҒӨгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜ deepwiki MCP гҒ§ https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official гҒ® hookify гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ гӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

е…Ёгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®еҶ…е®№гӮ’зңҒз•ҘгҒӣгҒҡгҒ«е ұе‘ҠгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ

content.content:
1вҶ’---
     2вҶ’description: Create hooks to prevent unwanted behaviors from conversation analysis or explicit instructions
     3вҶ’argument-hint: Optional specific behavior to address
     4вҶ’allowed-tools: ["Read", "Write", "AskUserQuestion", "Task", "Grep", "TodoWrite", "Skill"]
     5вҶ’---
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’# Hookify - Create Hooks from Unwanted Behaviors
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’**FIRST: Load the hookify:writing-rules skill** using the Skill tool to understand rule file format and syntax.
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’Create hook rules to prevent problematic behaviors by analyzing the conversation or from explicit user instructions.
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’## Your Task
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’You will help the user create hookify rules to prevent unwanted behaviors. Follow these steps:
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’### Step 1: Gather Behavior Information
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’**If $ARGUMENTS is provided:**
    20вҶ’- User has given specific instructions: `$ARGUMENTS`
    21вҶ’- Still analyze recent conversation (last 10-15 user messages) for additional context
    22вҶ’- Look for examples of the behavior happening
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’**If $ARGUMENTS is empty:**
    25вҶ’- Launch the conversation-analyzer agent to find problematic behaviors
    26вҶ’- Agent will scan user prompts for frustration signals
    27вҶ’- Agent will return structured findings
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’**To analyze conversation:**
    30вҶ’Use the Task tool to launch conversation-analyzer agent:
    31вҶ’```
    32вҶ’{
    33вҶ’  "subagent_type": "general-purpose",
    34вҶ’  "description": "Analyze conversation for unwanted behaviors",
    35вҶ’  "prompt": "You are analyzing a Claude Code conversation to find behaviors the user wants to prevent.
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’Read user messages in the current conversation and identify:
    38вҶ’1. Explicit requests to avoid something (\"don't do X\", \"stop doing Y\")
    39вҶ’2. Corrections or reversions (user fixing Claude's actions)
    40вҶ’3. Frustrated reactions (\"why did you do X?\", \"I didn't ask for that\")
    41вҶ’4. Repeated issues (same problem multiple times)
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’For each issue found, extract:
    44вҶ’- What tool was used (Bash, Edit, Write, etc.)
    45вҶ’- Specific pattern or command
    46вҶ’- Why it was problematic
    47вҶ’- User's stated reason
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’Return findings as a structured list with:
    50вҶ’- category: Type of issue
    51вҶ’- tool: Which tool was involved
    52вҶ’- pattern: Regex or literal pattern to match
    53вҶ’- context: What happened
    54вҶ’- severity: high/medium/low
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’Focus on the most recent issues (last 20-30 messages). Don't go back further unless explicitly asked."
    57вҶ’}
    58вҶ’```
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’### Step 2: Present Findings to User
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’After gathering behaviors (from arguments or agent), present to user using AskUserQuestion:
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’**Question 1: Which behaviors to hookify?**
    65вҶ’- Header: "Create Rules"
    66вҶ’- multiSelect: true
    67вҶ’- Options: List each detected behavior (max 4)
    68вҶ’  - Label: Short description (e.g., "Block rm -rf")
    69вҶ’  - Description: Why it's problematic
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’**Question 2: For each selected behavior, ask about action:**
    72вҶ’- "Should this block the operation or just warn?"
    73вҶ’- Options:
    74вҶ’  - "Just warn" (action: warn - shows message but allows)
    75вҶ’  - "Block operation" (action: block - prevents execution)
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’**Question 3: Ask for example patterns:**
    78вҶ’- "What patterns should trigger this rule?"
    79вҶ’- Show detected patterns
    80вҶ’- Allow user to refine or add more
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’### Step 3: Generate Rule Files
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’For each confirmed behavior, create a `.claude/hookify.{rule-name}.local.md` file:
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’**Rule naming convention:**
    87вҶ’- Use kebab-case
    88вҶ’- Be descriptive: `block-dangerous-rm`, `warn-console-log`, `require-tests-before-stop`
    89вҶ’- Start with action verb: block, warn, prevent, require
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’**File format:**
    92вҶ’```markdown
    93вҶ’---
    94вҶ’name: {rule-name}
    95вҶ’enabled: true
    96вҶ’event: {bash|file|stop|prompt|all}
    97вҶ’pattern: {regex pattern}
    98вҶ’action: {warn|block}
    99вҶ’---
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’{Message to show Claude when rule triggers}
   102вҶ’```
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’**Action values:**
   105вҶ’- `warn`: Show message but allow operation (default)
   106вҶ’- `block`: Prevent operation or stop session
   107вҶ’
   108вҶ’**For more complex rules (multiple conditions):**
   109вҶ’```markdown
   110вҶ’---
   111вҶ’name: {rule-name}
   112вҶ’enabled: true
   113вҶ’event: file
   114вҶ’conditions:
   115вҶ’  - field: file_path
   116вҶ’    operator: regex_match
   117вҶ’    pattern: \.env$
   118вҶ’  - field: new_text
   119вҶ’    operator: contains
   120вҶ’    pattern: API_KEY
   121вҶ’---
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’{Warning message}
   124вҶ’```
   125вҶ’
   126вҶ’### Step 4: Create Files and Confirm
   127вҶ’
   128вҶ’**IMPORTANT**: Rule files must be created in the current working directory's `.claude/` folder, NOT the plugin directory.
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’Use the current working directory (where Claude Code was started) as the base path.
   131вҶ’
   132вҶ’1. Check if `.claude/` directory exists in current working directory
   133вҶ’   - If not, create it first with: `mkdir -p .claude`
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’2. Use Write tool to create each `.claude/hookify.{name}.local.md` file
   136вҶ’   - Use relative path from current working directory: `.claude/hookify.{name}.local.md`
   137вҶ’   - The path should resolve to the project's .claude directory, not the plugin's
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’3. Show user what was created:
   140вҶ’   ```
   141вҶ’   Created 3 hookify rules:
   142вҶ’   - .claude/hookify.dangerous-rm.local.md
   143вҶ’   - .claude/hookify.console-log.local.md
   144вҶ’   - .claude/hookify.sensitive-files.local.md
   145вҶ’
   146вҶ’   These rules will trigger on:
   147вҶ’   - dangerous-rm: Bash commands matching "rm -rf"
   148вҶ’   - console-log: Edits adding console.log statements
   149вҶ’   - sensitive-files: Edits to .env or credentials files
   150вҶ’   ```
   151вҶ’
   152вҶ’4. Verify files were created in the correct location by listing them
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’5. Inform user: **"Rules are active immediately - no restart needed!"**
   155вҶ’
   156вҶ’   The hookify hooks are already loaded and will read your new rules on the next tool use.
   157вҶ’
   158вҶ’## Event Types Reference
   159вҶ’
   160вҶ’- **bash**: Matches Bash tool commands
   161вҶ’- **file**: Matches Edit, Write, MultiEdit tools
   162вҶ’- **stop**: Matches when agent wants to stop (use for completion checks)
   163вҶ’- **prompt**: Matches when user submits prompts
   164вҶ’- **all**: Matches all events
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’## Pattern Writing Tips
   167вҶ’
   168вҶ’**Bash patterns:**
   169вҶ’- Match dangerous commands: `rm\s+-rf|chmod\s+777|dd\s+if=`
   170вҶ’- Match specific tools: `npm\s+install\s+|pip\s+install`
   171вҶ’
   172вҶ’**File patterns:**
   173вҶ’- Match code patterns: `console\.log\(|eval\(|innerHTML\s*=`
   174вҶ’- Match file paths: `\.env$|\.git/|node_modules/`
   175вҶ’
   176вҶ’**Stop patterns:**
   177вҶ’- Check for missing steps: (check transcript or completion criteria)
   178вҶ’
   179вҶ’## Example Workflow
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’**User says**: "/hookify Don't use rm -rf without asking me first"
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’**Your response**:
   184вҶ’1. Analyze: User wants to prevent rm -rf commands
   185вҶ’2. Ask: "Should I block this command or just warn you?"
   186вҶ’3. User selects: "Just warn"
   187вҶ’4. Create `.claude/hookify.dangerous-rm.local.md`:
   188вҶ’   ```markdown
   189вҶ’   ---
   190вҶ’   name: warn-dangerous-rm
   191вҶ’   enabled: true
   192вҶ’   event: bash
   193вҶ’   pattern: rm\s+-rf
   194вҶ’   ---
   195вҶ’
   196вҶ’   вҡ пёҸ **Dangerous rm command detected**
   197вҶ’
   198вҶ’   You requested to be warned before using rm -rf.
   199вҶ’   Please verify the path is correct.
   200вҶ’   ```
   201вҶ’5. Confirm: "Created hookify rule. It's active immediately - try triggering it!"
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’## Important Notes
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’- **No restart needed**: Rules take effect immediately on the next tool use
   206вҶ’- **File location**: Create files in project's `.claude/` directory (current working directory), NOT the plugin's .claude/
   207вҶ’- **Regex syntax**: Use Python regex syntax (raw strings, no need to escape in YAML)
   208вҶ’- **Action types**: Rules can `warn` (default) or `block` operations
   209вҶ’- **Testing**: Test rules immediately after creating them
   210вҶ’
   211вҶ’## Troubleshooting
   212вҶ’
   213вҶ’**If rule file creation fails:**
   214вҶ’1. Check current working directory with pwd
   215вҶ’2. Ensure `.claude/` directory exists (create with mkdir if needed)
   216вҶ’3. Use absolute path if needed: `{cwd}/.claude/hookify.{name}.local.md`
   217вҶ’4. Verify file was created with Glob or ls
   218вҶ’
   219вҶ’**If rule doesn't trigger after creation:**
   220вҶ’1. Verify file is in project `.claude/` not plugin `.claude/`
   221вҶ’2. Check file with Read tool to ensure pattern is correct
   222вҶ’3. Test pattern with: `python3 -c "import re; print(re.search(r'pattern', 'test text'))"`
   223вҶ’4. Verify `enabled: true` in frontmatter
   224вҶ’5. Remember: Rules work immediately, no restart needed
   225вҶ’
   226вҶ’**If blocking seems too strict:**
   227вҶ’1. Change `action: block` to `action: warn` in the rule file
   228вҶ’2. Or adjust the pattern to be more specific
   229вҶ’3. Changes take effect on next tool use
   230вҶ’
   231вҶ’Use TodoWrite to track your progress through the steps.
   232вҶ’

1вҶ’---
     2вҶ’description: List all configured hookify rules
     3вҶ’allowed-tools: ["Glob", "Read", "Skill"]
     4вҶ’---
     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’# List Hookify Rules
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’**Load hookify:writing-rules skill first** to understand rule format.
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’Show all configured hookify rules in the project.
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’## Steps
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’1. Use Glob tool to find all hookify rule files:
    15вҶ’   ```
    16вҶ’   pattern: ".claude/hookify.*.local.md"
    17вҶ’   ```
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’2. For each file found:
    20вҶ’   - Use Read tool to read the file
    21вҶ’   - Extract frontmatter fields: name, enabled, event, pattern
    22вҶ’   - Extract message preview (first 100 chars)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’3. Present results in a table:
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’```
    27вҶ’## Configured Hookify Rules
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’| Name | Enabled | Event | Pattern | File |
    30вҶ’|------|---------|-------|---------|------|
    31вҶ’| warn-dangerous-rm | вң… Yes | bash | rm\s+-rf | hookify.dangerous-rm.local.md |
    32вҶ’| warn-console-log | вң… Yes | file | console\.log\( | hookify.console-log.local.md |
    33вҶ’| check-tests | вқҢ No | stop | .* | hookify.require-tests.local.md |
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’**Total**: 3 rules (2 enabled, 1 disabled)
    36вҶ’```
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’4. For each rule, show a brief preview:
    39вҶ’```
    40вҶ’### warn-dangerous-rm
    41вҶ’**Event**: bash
    42вҶ’**Pattern**: `rm\s+-rf`
    43вҶ’**Message**: "вҡ пёҸ **Dangerous rm command detected!** This command could delete..."
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’**Status**: вң… Active
    46вҶ’**File**: .claude/hookify.dangerous-rm.local.md
    47вҶ’```
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’5. Add helpful footer:
    50вҶ’```
    51вҶ’---
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’To modify a rule: Edit the .local.md file directly
    54вҶ’To disable a rule: Set `enabled: false` in frontmatter
    55вҶ’To enable a rule: Set `enabled: true` in frontmatter
    56вҶ’To delete a rule: Remove the .local.md file
    57вҶ’To create a rule: Use `/hookify` command
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’**Remember**: Changes take effect immediately - no restart needed
    60вҶ’```
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’## If No Rules Found
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’If no hookify rules exist:
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’```
    67вҶ’## No Hookify Rules Configured
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’You haven't created any hookify rules yet.
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’To get started:
    72вҶ’1. Use `/hookify` to analyze conversation and create rules
    73вҶ’2. Or manually create `.claude/hookify.my-rule.local.md` files
    74вҶ’3. See `/hookify:help` for documentation
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’Example:
    77вҶ’```
    78вҶ’/hookify Warn me when I use console.log
    79вҶ’```
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’Check `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/examples/` for example rule files.
    82вҶ’```
    83вҶ’
