content.content:
> ## Documentation Index
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://code.claude.com/docs/llms.txt
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

# Create plugins

> Create custom plugins to extend Claude Code with skills, agents, hooks, and MCP servers.

Plugins let you extend Claude Code with custom functionality that can be shared across projects and teams. This guide covers creating your own plugins with skills, agents, hooks, and MCP servers.

Looking to install existing plugins? See [Discover and install plugins](/en/discover-plugins). For complete technical specifications, see [Plugins reference](/en/plugins-reference).

## When to use plugins vs standalone configuration

Claude Code supports two ways to add custom skills, agents, and hooks:

| Approach                                                    | Skill names          | Best for                                                                                        |
| :---------------------------------------------------------- | :------------------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Standalone** (`.claude/` directory)                       | `/hello`             | Personal workflows, project-specific customizations, quick experiments                          |
| **Plugins** (directories with `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`) | `/plugin-name:hello` | Sharing with teammates, distributing to community, versioned releases, reusable across projects |

**Use standalone configuration when**:

* You're customizing Claude Code for a single project
* The configuration is personal and doesn't need to be shared
* You're experimenting with skills or hooks before packaging them
* You want short skill names like `/hello` or `/review`

**Use plugins when**:

* You want to share functionality with your team or community
* You need the same skills/agents across multiple projects
* You want version control and easy updates for your extensions
* You're distributing through a marketplace
* You're okay with namespaced skills like `/my-plugin:hello` (namespacing prevents conflicts between plugins)

<Tip>
  Start with standalone configuration in `.claude/` for quick iteration, then [convert to a plugin](#convert-existing-configurations-to-plugins) when you're ready to share.
</Tip>

## Quickstart

This quickstart walks you through creating a plugin with a custom skill. You'll create a manifest (the configuration file that defines your plugin), add a skill, and test it locally using the `--plugin-dir` flag.

### Prerequisites

* Claude Code [installed and authenticated](/en/quickstart#step-1-install-claude-code)
* Claude Code version 1.0.33 or later (run `claude --version` to check)

<Note>
  If you don't see the `/plugin` command, update Claude Code to the latest version. See [Troubleshooting](/en/troubleshooting) for upgrade instructions.
</Note>

### Create your first plugin

<Steps>
  <Step title="Create the plugin directory">
    Every plugin lives in its own directory containing a manifest and your skills, agents, or hooks. Create one now:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    mkdir my-first-plugin
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create the plugin manifest">
    The manifest file at `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` defines your plugin's identity: its name, description, and version. Claude Code uses this metadata to display your plugin in the plugin manager.

    Create the `.claude-plugin` directory inside your plugin folder:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    mkdir my-first-plugin/.claude-plugin
    ```

    Then create `my-first-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` with this content:

    ```json my-first-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json theme={null}
    {
    "name": "my-first-plugin",
    "description": "A greeting plugin to learn the basics",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "author": {
    "name": "Your Name"
    }
    }
    ```

    | Field         | Purpose                                                                                                |
    | :------------ | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    | `name`        | Unique identifier and skill namespace. Skills are prefixed with this (e.g., `/my-first-plugin:hello`). |
    | `description` | Shown in the plugin manager when browsing or installing plugins.                                       |
    | `version`     | Track releases using [semantic versioning](/en/plugins-reference#version-management).                  |
    | `author`      | Optional. Helpful for attribution.                                                                     |

    For additional fields like `homepage`, `repository`, and `license`, see the [full manifest schema](/en/plugins-reference#plugin-manifest-schema).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add a skill">
    Skills live in the `skills/` directory. Each skill is a folder containing a `SKILL.md` file. The folder name becomes the skill name, prefixed with the plugin's namespace (`hello/` in a plugin named `my-first-plugin` creates `/my-first-plugin:hello`).

    Create a skill directory in your plugin folder:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    mkdir -p my-first-plugin/skills/hello
    ```

    Then create `my-first-plugin/skills/hello/SKILL.md` with this content:

    ```markdown my-first-plugin/skills/hello/SKILL.md theme={null}
    ---
    description: Greet the user with a friendly message
    disable-model-invocation: true
    ---

    Greet the user warmly and ask how you can help them today.
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Test your plugin">
    Run Claude Code with the `--plugin-dir` flag to load your plugin:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    claude --plugin-dir ./my-first-plugin
    ```

    Once Claude Code starts, try your new command:

    ```shell  theme={null}
    /my-first-plugin:hello
    ```

    You'll see Claude respond with a greeting. Run `/help` to see your command listed under the plugin namespace.

    <Note>
      **Why namespacing?** Plugin skills are always namespaced (like `/greet:hello`) to prevent conflicts when multiple plugins have skills with the same name.

      To change the namespace prefix, update the `name` field in `plugin.json`.
    </Note>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add skill arguments">
    Make your skill dynamic by accepting user input. The `$ARGUMENTS` placeholder captures any text the user provides after the skill name.

    Update your `hello.md` file:

    ```markdown my-first-plugin/commands/hello.md theme={null}
    ---
    description: Greet the user with a personalized message
    ---

    # Hello Command

    Greet the user named "$ARGUMENTS" warmly and ask how you can help them today. Make the greeting personal and encouraging.
    ```

    Restart Claude Code to pick up the changes, then try the command with your name:

    ```shell  theme={null}
    /my-first-plugin:hello Alex
    ```

    Claude will greet you by name. For more on passing arguments to skills, see [Skills](/en/skills#pass-arguments-to-skills).
  </Step>
</Steps>

You've successfully created and tested a plugin with these key components:

* **Plugin manifest** (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json`): describes your plugin's metadata
* **Commands directory** (`commands/`): contains your custom skills
* **Skill arguments** (`$ARGUMENTS`): captures user input for dynamic behavior

<Tip>
  The `--plugin-dir` flag is useful for development and testing. When you're ready to share your plugin with others, see [Create and distribute a plugin marketplace](/en/plugin-marketplaces).
</Tip>

## Plugin structure overview

You've created a plugin with a skill, but plugins can include much more: custom agents, hooks, MCP servers, and LSP servers.

<Warning>
  **Common mistake**: Don't put `commands/`, `agents/`, `skills/`, or `hooks/` inside the `.claude-plugin/` directory. Only `plugin.json` goes inside `.claude-plugin/`. All other directories must be at the plugin root level.
</Warning>

| Directory         | Location    | Purpose                                                                        |
| :---------------- | :---------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `.claude-plugin/` | Plugin root | Contains `plugin.json` manifest (optional if components use default locations) |
| `commands/`       | Plugin root | Skills as Markdown files                                                       |
| `agents/`         | Plugin root | Custom agent definitions                                                       |
| `skills/`         | Plugin root | Agent Skills with `SKILL.md` files                                             |
| `hooks/`          | Plugin root | Event handlers in `hooks.json`                                                 |
| `.mcp.json`       | Plugin root | MCP server configurations                                                      |
| `.lsp.json`       | Plugin root | LSP server configurations for code intelligence                                |

<Note>
  **Next steps**: Ready to add more features? Jump to [Develop more complex plugins](#develop-more-complex-plugins) to add agents, hooks, MCP servers, and LSP servers. For complete technical specifications of all plugin components, see [Plugins reference](/en/plugins-reference).
</Note>

## Develop more complex plugins

Once you're comfortable with basic plugins, you can create more sophisticated extensions.

### Add Skills to your plugin

Plugins can include [Agent Skills](/en/skills) to extend Claude's capabilities. Skills are model-invoked: Claude automatically uses them based on the task context.

Add a `skills/` directory at your plugin root with Skill folders containing `SKILL.md` files:

```
my-plugin/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ .claude-plugin/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ plugin.json
в””в”Җв”Җ skills/
    в””в”Җв”Җ code-review/
        в””в”Җв”Җ SKILL.md
```

Each `SKILL.md` needs frontmatter with `name` and `description` fields, followed by instructions:

```yaml  theme={null}
---
name: code-review
description: Reviews code for best practices and potential issues. Use when reviewing code, checking PRs, or analyzing code quality.
---

When reviewing code, check for:
1. Code organization and structure
2. Error handling
3. Security concerns
4. Test coverage
```

After installing the plugin, restart Claude Code to load the Skills. For complete Skill authoring guidance including progressive disclosure and tool restrictions, see [Agent Skills](/en/skills).

### Add LSP servers to your plugin

<Tip>
  For common languages like TypeScript, Python, and Rust, install the pre-built LSP plugins from the official marketplace. Create custom LSP plugins only when you need support for languages not already covered.
</Tip>

LSP (Language Server Protocol) plugins give Claude real-time code intelligence. If you need to support a language that doesn't have an official LSP plugin, you can create your own by adding an `.lsp.json` file to your plugin:

```json .lsp.json theme={null}
{
  "go": {
    "command": "gopls",
    "args": ["serve"],
    "extensionToLanguage": {
      ".go": "go"
    }
  }
}
```

Users installing your plugin must have the language server binary installed on their machine.

For complete LSP configuration options, see [LSP servers](/en/plugins-reference#lsp-servers).

### Organize complex plugins

For plugins with many components, organize your directory structure by functionality. For complete directory layouts and organization patterns, see [Plugin directory structure](/en/plugins-reference#plugin-directory-structure).

### Test your plugins locally

Use the `--plugin-dir` flag to test plugins during development. This loads your plugin directly without requiring installation.

```bash  theme={null}
claude --plugin-dir ./my-plugin
```

As you make changes to your plugin, restart Claude Code to pick up the updates. Test your plugin components:

* Try your commands with `/command-name`
* Check that agents appear in `/agents`
* Verify hooks work as expected

<Tip>
  You can load multiple plugins at once by specifying the flag multiple times:

  ```bash  theme={null}
  claude --plugin-dir ./plugin-one --plugin-dir ./plugin-two
  ```
</Tip>

### Debug plugin issues

If your plugin isn't working as expected:

1. **Check the structure**: Ensure your directories are at the plugin root, not inside `.claude-plugin/`
2. **Test components individually**: Check each command, agent, and hook separately
3. **Use validation and debugging tools**: See [Debugging and development tools](/en/plugins-reference#debugging-and-development-tools) for CLI commands and troubleshooting techniques

### Share your plugins

When your plugin is ready to share:

1. **Add documentation**: Include a `README.md` with installation and usage instructions
2. **Version your plugin**: Use [semantic versioning](/en/plugins-reference#version-management) in your `plugin.json`
3. **Create or use a marketplace**: Distribute through [plugin marketplaces](/en/plugin-marketplaces) for installation
4. **Test with others**: Have team members test the plugin before wider distribution

Once your plugin is in a marketplace, others can install it using the instructions in [Discover and install plugins](/en/discover-plugins).

<Note>
  For complete technical specifications, debugging techniques, and distribution strategies, see [Plugins reference](/en/plugins-reference).
</Note>

## Convert existing configurations to plugins

If you already have skills or hooks in your `.claude/` directory, you can convert them into a plugin for easier sharing and distribution.

### Migration steps

<Steps>
  <Step title="Create the plugin structure">
    Create a new plugin directory:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    mkdir -p my-plugin/.claude-plugin
    ```

    Create the manifest file at `my-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`:

    ```json my-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json theme={null}
    {
      "name": "my-plugin",
      "description": "Migrated from standalone configuration",
      "version": "1.0.0"
    }
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Copy your existing files">
    Copy your existing configurations to the plugin directory:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    # Copy commands
    cp -r .claude/commands my-plugin/

    # Copy agents (if any)
    cp -r .claude/agents my-plugin/

    # Copy skills (if any)
    cp -r .claude/skills my-plugin/
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Migrate hooks">
    If you have hooks in your settings, create a hooks directory:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    mkdir my-plugin/hooks
    ```

    Create `my-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` with your hooks configuration. Copy the `hooks` object from your `.claude/settings.json` or `settings.local.json`, since the format is the same. The command receives hook input as JSON on stdin, so use `jq` to extract the file path:

    ```json my-plugin/hooks/hooks.json theme={null}
    {
      "hooks": {
        "PostToolUse": [
          {
            "matcher": "Write|Edit",
            "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | xargs npm run lint:fix" }]
          }
        ]
      }
    }
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Test your migrated plugin">
    Load your plugin to verify everything works:

    ```bash  theme={null}
    claude --plugin-dir ./my-plugin
    ```

    Test each component: run your commands, check agents appear in `/agents`, and verify hooks trigger correctly.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### What changes when migrating

| Standalone (`.claude/`)       | Plugin                           |
| :---------------------------- | :------------------------------- |
| Only available in one project | Can be shared via marketplaces   |
| Files in `.claude/commands/`  | Files in `plugin-name/commands/` |
| Hooks in `settings.json`      | Hooks in `hooks/hooks.json`      |
| Must manually copy to share   | Install with `/plugin install`   |

<Note>
  After migrating, you can remove the original files from `.claude/` to avoid duplicates. The plugin version will take precedence when loaded.
</Note>

## Next steps

Now that you understand Claude Code's plugin system, here are suggested paths for different goals:

### For plugin users

* [Discover and install plugins](/en/discover-plugins): browse marketplaces and install plugins
* [Configure team marketplaces](/en/discover-plugins#configure-team-marketplaces): set up repository-level plugins for your team

### For plugin developers

* [Create and distribute a marketplace](/en/plugin-marketplaces): package and share your plugins
* [Plugins reference](/en/plugins-reference): complete technical specifications
* Dive deeper into specific plugin components:
  * [Skills](/en/skills): skill development details
  * [Subagents](/en/sub-agents): agent configuration and capabilities
  * [Hooks](/en/hooks): event handling and automation
  * [MCP](/en/mcp): external tool integration

data.prompt:
Claude Code гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҒ«гҒҠгҒ‘гӮӢ skill гҒЁ command гҒ®йҒ•гҒ„гӮ’ж•ҷгҒҲгҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮзү№гҒ«:
1. command гӮ’ skill гҒ«з§»иЎҢгҒҷгӮӢе ҙеҗҲгҖҒallowed-tools гҒҜгҒ©гҒҶгҒӘгӮӢгҒӢ
2. skill гҒ® frontmatter гҒ§ allowed-tools гҒҜдҪҝгҒҲгӮӢгҒӢ
3. skill гҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ  (SKILL.md гҒ®й…ҚзҪ®)
4. skill гҒ® description (гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјжқЎд»¶) гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№гҒ®гғҷгӮ№гғҲгғ—гғ©гӮҜгғҶгӮЈгӮ№

content.text:
е®Ңз’§гҒ§гҒҷгҖӮе…¬ејҸгғүгӮӯгғҘгғЎгғігғҲгҒӢгӮү skill гҒЁ command гҒ®йҒ•гҒ„гҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰжҳҺзўәгҒӘжғ…е ұгҒҢеҫ—гӮүгӮҢгҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮгҒӮгҒӘгҒҹгҒ® 4 гҒӨгҒ®иіӘе•ҸгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢи©ізҙ°гҒӘеӣһзӯ”гӮ’гҒҫгҒЁгӮҒгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ

## Skill гҒЁ Command гҒ®йҒ•гҒ„

гҒҫгҒҡеүҚжҸҗгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰпјҡ**`command` гҒЁ `skill` гҒҜзөұеҗҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ**

> **Custom slash commands have been merged into skills.** A file at `.claude/commands/review.md` and a skill at `.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md` both create `/review` and work the same way. Your existing `.claude/commands/` files keep working. Skills add optional features: a directory for supporting files, frontmatter to control whether you or Claude invokes them, and the ability for Claude to load them automatically when relevant.

гҒӨгҒҫгӮҠпјҡ
- `.claude/commands/review.md` гҒЁ `.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md` гҒҜ**ж©ҹиғҪзҡ„гҒ«еҗҢзӯү**
- Skill гҒҜ Command гҒ®дёҠдҪҚдә’жҸӣгҒ§гҖҒд»ҘдёӢгҒ®иҝҪеҠ ж©ҹиғҪгӮ’жҢҒгҒӨ
  - е°Ӯз”ЁгғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘеҶ…гҒ®гӮөгғқгғјгғҲгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«
  - гӮҲгӮҠи©ізҙ°гҒӘ frontmatter гӮігғігғҲгғӯгғјгғ«
  - иҮӘеӢ•гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјж©ҹиғҪ

---

## 1. Command гӮ’ Skill гҒ«з§»иЎҢгҒҷгӮӢе ҙеҗҲгҖҒallowed-tools гҒҜгҒ©гҒҶгҒӘгӮӢгҒӢ

**Skill гҒ® frontmatter гҒ§ `allowed-tools` гҒҢдҪҝз”ЁеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ**

е…¬ејҸгғүгӮӯгғҘгғЎгғігғҲгӮҲгӮҠпјҡ

```yaml
---
name: safe-reader
description: Read files without making changes
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob
---
```

з§»иЎҢжҷӮгҒ®еҜҫеҝңпјҡ
- `.claude/commands/xxx.md` гҒ« `allowed-tools` гҒҢиЁҳијүгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҹе ҙеҗҲгҒҜгҖҒгҒқгҒ®гҒҫгҒҫ `.claude/skills/xxx/SKILL.md` гҒ® frontmatter гҒ«иЁҳијүгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„
- **гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒҜеӨүгӮҸгӮҠгҒҫгҒӣгӮ“** (еҗҢгҒҳгғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгӮ’дҪҝз”Ё)

---

## 2. Skill гҒ® frontmatter гҒ§ allowed-tools гҒҜдҪҝгҒҲгӮӢгҒӢ

**гҒҜгҒ„гҖҒдҪҝгҒҲгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ**

Skill frontmatter гҒ®е®Ңе…ЁгҒӘгғӘгғ•гӮЎгғ¬гғігӮ№пјҡ

| гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү                 | иӘ¬жҳҺ                                                                               |
|:---------------------------|:-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `name`                     | гӮ№гӮӯгғ«еҗҚ (гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲ: гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘеҗҚ)                                               |
| `description`              | гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒ®иӘ¬жҳҺгҒЁдҪҝз”ЁгӮҝгӮӨгғҹгғігӮ°пјҲ**жҺЁеҘЁ**пјү |
| `allowed-tools`            | **гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«е®ҹиЎҢжҷӮгҒ«з„ЎиЁұеҸҜгҒ§дҪҝз”ЁгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгғ„гғјгғ«**                                      |
| `disable-model-invocation` | Claude гҒҢиҮӘеӢ•гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ§гҒҚгҒӘгҒ„гӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢ                                             |
| `user-invocable`           | гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢгғЎгғӢгғҘгғјгҒӢгӮүе‘јгҒіеҮәгҒӣгҒӘгҒ„гӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢ                                        |
| `argument-hint`            | еј•ж•°гғ’гғігғҲ (дҫӢ: `[issue-number]`)                                                   |
| `model`                    | гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«е®ҹиЎҢжҷӮгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгғўгғҮгғ«                                                    |
| `context`                  | `fork` гҒ§зӢ¬з«ӢгҒ—гҒҹ subagent гҒ§е®ҹиЎҢ                                                  |
| `agent`                    | `context: fork` жҷӮгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢ agent еһӢ (`Explore`, `Plan` гҒӘгҒ©)                     |
| `hooks`                    | гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гӮ№гӮігғјгғ—гҒ®гғ•гғғгӮҜе®ҡзҫ©                                                         |

дҪҝз”ЁдҫӢпјҡ
```yaml
---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the application to production
disable-model-invocation: true
allowed-tools: Bash(*)
---
```

---

## 3. Skill гҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ  (SKILL.md гҒ®й…ҚзҪ®)

**иӨҮж•°гҒ®гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒҢеӯҳеңЁгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ**

### еҹәжң¬гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі

```
.claude/skills/my-skill/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ SKILL.md          # еҝ…й ҲпјҡгғЎгӮӨгғіжҢҮзӨәжӣё
в”ңв”Җв”Җ reference.md      # гӮӘгғ—гӮ·гғ§гғіпјҡи©ізҙ°гғӘгғ•гӮЎгғ¬гғігӮ№
в”ңв”Җв”Җ examples.md       # гӮӘгғ—гӮ·гғ§гғіпјҡдҪҝз”ЁдҫӢ
в””в”Җв”Җ scripts/
    в””в”Җв”Җ helper.py     # гӮӘгғ—гӮ·гғ§гғіпјҡе®ҹиЎҢгӮ№гӮҜгғӘгғ—гғҲ
```

### гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲе…ЁдҪ“гҒ§гҒ®ж§ӢйҖ 

| гғӯгӮұгғјгӮ·гғ§гғі          | гғ‘гӮ№                                           | еҜҫиұЎзҜ„еӣІ                       |
|:---------------------|:----------------------------------------------|:------------------------------|
| Enterprise           | managed settings гҒ§жҢҮе®ҡ                         | зө„з№”е…ЁдҪ“                       |
| Personal (жҺЁеҘЁ)      | `~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`      | гҒҷгҒ№гҒҰгҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ           |
| **Project**          | `.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`        | **гҒ“гҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ®гҒҝ**       |
| Plugin               | `<plugin>/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`       | гғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғіжңүеҠ№жҷӮ               |

### гғҚгӮ№гғҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ§гҒ®иҮӘеӢ•зҷәиҰӢ

Claude Code гҒҜиҮӘеӢ•зҡ„гҒ«гғҚгӮ№гғҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹ `.claude/skills/` гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгӮ’зҷәиҰӢгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ

```
monorepo/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ .claude/skills/global-skill/SKILL.md
в””в”Җв”Җ packages/
    в””в”Җв”Җ frontend/
        в””в”Җв”Җ .claude/skills/frontend-skill/SKILL.md  # иҮӘеӢ•зҷәиҰӢгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ
```

## 4. Skill гҒ® description (гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјжқЎд»¶) гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№гҒ®гғҷгӮ№гғҲгғ—гғ©гӮҜгғҶгӮЈгӮ№

### йҮҚиҰҒгҒӘгғқгӮӨгғігғҲ

Claude гҒҜ skill гҒ® **description** гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒиҮӘеӢ•гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ®еҲӨж–ӯгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ

> Claude uses this to decide when to apply the skill.

### гғҷгӮ№гғҲгғ—гғ©гӮҜгғҶгӮЈгӮ№

**1. гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢе®ҹйҡӣгҒ«дҪҝгҒҶиЁҖи‘үгӮ’еҗ«гӮҒгӮӢ**

```yaml
---
description: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
---
```

**2. иӨҮж•°гҒ®гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгӮ·гғҠгғӘгӮӘгӮ’еҲ—жҢҷ**

```yaml
---
description: Deploy the application to production. Use when you need to deploy, release a new version, or push changes to production.
---
```

**3. е…·дҪ“зҡ„гҒӘгӮӯгғјгғҜгғјгғүгӮ’еҗ«гӮҒгӮӢ**

- гҖҢexplanationгҖҚгҖҢexplainгҖҚгҖҢhow does it workгҖҚ
- гҖҢdeployгҖҚгҖҢreleaseгҖҚгҖҢproductionгҖҚ
- гҖҢreviewгҖҚгҖҢcode reviewгҖҚгҖҢquality checkгҖҚ

**4. жӣ–жҳ§гҒӘиӘ¬жҳҺгҒҜйҒҝгҒ‘гӮӢ**

```yaml
# вқҢ жӮӘгҒ„дҫӢ
description: Helpful skill

# вң… иүҜгҒ„дҫӢ
description: Reviews code for best practices and potential issues. Use when reviewing code, checking PRs, or analyzing code quality.
```

### еҸӮиҖғдҫӢ

е…¬ејҸгғүгӮӯгғҘгғЎгғігғҲгҒ®дҫӢгӮҲгӮҠпјҡ

```yaml
---
name: explain-code
description: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
---
```

```yaml
---
name: deep-research
description: Research a topic thoroughly
context: fork
agent: Explore
---
```

### гғҲгғ©гғ–гғ«гӮ·гғҘгғјгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°

гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҢгғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ•гӮҢгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲпјҡ

1. **description гҒ«гӮӯгғјгғҜгғјгғүгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҒӢзўәиӘҚ**
2. **гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҢ `/` гғЎгғӢгғҘгғјгҒ«еҮәгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒӢзўәиӘҚ** (`What skills are available?` гҒ§зўәиӘҚ)
3. **гғӘгӮҜгӮЁгӮ№гғҲгӮ’ description гҒ«гғһгғғгғҒгҒ•гҒӣгҒҰгғӘгғ•гғ¬гғјгӮә**
4. **зӣҙжҺҘе‘јгҒіеҮәгҒ—** `/skill-name` гҒ§еј·еҲ¶е®ҹиЎҢ

---

## е®ҹи·өдҫӢпјҡCommand гҒӢгӮү Skill гҒёгҒ®з§»иЎҢ

### з§»иЎҢеүҚ (`.claude/commands/review.md`)

Code review instructions...

description: Reviews code for best practices and potential issues. Use when reviewing code, checking PRs, or analyzing code quality.
allowed-tools: Read, Grep
disable-model-invocation: false  # Claude гҒҢиҮӘеӢ•гҒ§дҪҝз”ЁеҸҜиғҪ

Code review instructions...
```

е®Ңз’§гҒ§гҒҷгҖӮе…¬ејҸгғүгӮӯгғҘгғЎгғігғҲгҒӢгӮү skill гҒЁ command гҒ®йҒ•гҒ„гҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰжҳҺзўәгҒӘжғ…е ұгҒҢеҫ—гӮүгӮҢгҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮгҒӮгҒӘгҒҹгҒ® 4 гҒӨгҒ®иіӘе•ҸгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢи©ізҙ°гҒӘеӣһзӯ”гӮ’гҒҫгҒЁгӮҒгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ

## Skill гҒЁ Command гҒ®йҒ•гҒ„

гҒҫгҒҡеүҚжҸҗгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰпјҡ**`command` гҒЁ `skill` гҒҜзөұеҗҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ**

> **Custom slash commands have been merged into skills.** A file at `.claude/commands/review.md` and a skill at `.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md` both create `/review` and work the same way. Your existing `.claude/commands/` files keep working. Skills add optional features: a directory for supporting files, frontmatter to control whether you or Claude invokes them, and the ability for Claude to load them automatically when relevant.

гҒӨгҒҫгӮҠпјҡ
- `.claude/commands/review.md` гҒЁ `.claude/skills/review/SKILL.md` гҒҜ**ж©ҹиғҪзҡ„гҒ«еҗҢзӯү**
- Skill гҒҜ Command гҒ®дёҠдҪҚдә’жҸӣгҒ§гҖҒд»ҘдёӢгҒ®иҝҪеҠ ж©ҹиғҪгӮ’жҢҒгҒӨ
  - е°Ӯз”ЁгғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘеҶ…гҒ®гӮөгғқгғјгғҲгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«
  - гӮҲгӮҠи©ізҙ°гҒӘ frontmatter гӮігғігғҲгғӯгғјгғ«
  - иҮӘеӢ•гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјж©ҹиғҪ

---

## 1. Command гӮ’ Skill гҒ«з§»иЎҢгҒҷгӮӢе ҙеҗҲгҖҒallowed-tools гҒҜгҒ©гҒҶгҒӘгӮӢгҒӢ

**Skill гҒ® frontmatter гҒ§ `allowed-tools` гҒҢдҪҝз”ЁеҸҜиғҪгҒ§гҒҷгҖӮ**

е…¬ејҸгғүгӮӯгғҘгғЎгғігғҲгӮҲгӮҠпјҡ

```yaml
---
name: safe-reader
description: Read files without making changes
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob
---
```

з§»иЎҢжҷӮгҒ®еҜҫеҝңпјҡ
- `.claude/commands/xxx.md` гҒ« `allowed-tools` гҒҢиЁҳијүгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҹе ҙеҗҲгҒҜгҖҒгҒқгҒ®гҒҫгҒҫ `.claude/skills/xxx/SKILL.md` гҒ® frontmatter гҒ«иЁҳијүгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„
- **гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒҜеӨүгӮҸгӮҠгҒҫгҒӣгӮ“** (еҗҢгҒҳгғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгӮ’дҪҝз”Ё)

## 2. Skill гҒ® frontmatter гҒ§ allowed-tools гҒҜдҪҝгҒҲгӮӢгҒӢ

**гҒҜгҒ„гҖҒдҪҝгҒҲгҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ**

Skill frontmatter гҒ®е®Ңе…ЁгҒӘгғӘгғ•гӮЎгғ¬гғігӮ№пјҡ

| гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү                 | иӘ¬жҳҺ                                                                               |
|:---------------------------|:-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `name`                     | гӮ№гӮӯгғ«еҗҚ (гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲ: гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘеҗҚ)                                               |
| `description`              | гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒ®иӘ¬жҳҺгҒЁдҪҝз”ЁгӮҝгӮӨгғҹгғігӮ°пјҲ**жҺЁеҘЁ**пјү |
| `allowed-tools`            | **гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«е®ҹиЎҢжҷӮгҒ«з„ЎиЁұеҸҜгҒ§дҪҝз”ЁгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгғ„гғјгғ«**                                      |
| `disable-model-invocation` | Claude гҒҢиҮӘеӢ•гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ§гҒҚгҒӘгҒ„гӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢ                                             |
| `user-invocable`           | гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢгғЎгғӢгғҘгғјгҒӢгӮүе‘јгҒіеҮәгҒӣгҒӘгҒ„гӮҲгҒҶгҒ«гҒҷгӮӢ                                        |
| `argument-hint`            | еј•ж•°гғ’гғігғҲ (дҫӢ: `[issue-number]`)                                                   |
| `model`                    | гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«е®ҹиЎҢжҷӮгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгғўгғҮгғ«                                                    |
| `context`                  | `fork` гҒ§зӢ¬з«ӢгҒ—гҒҹ subagent гҒ§е®ҹиЎҢ                                                  |
| `agent`                    | `context: fork` жҷӮгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢ agent еһӢ (`Explore`, `Plan` гҒӘгҒ©)                     |
| `hooks`                    | гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гӮ№гӮігғјгғ—гҒ®гғ•гғғгӮҜе®ҡзҫ©                                                         |

дҪҝз”ЁдҫӢпјҡ
```yaml
---
name: deploy
description: Deploy the application to production
disable-model-invocation: true
allowed-tools: Bash(*)
---
```

---

## 3. Skill гҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘж§ӢйҖ  (SKILL.md гҒ®й…ҚзҪ®)

**иӨҮж•°гҒ®гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒҢеӯҳеңЁгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ**

```

в””в”Җв”Җ packages/
    в””в”Җв”Җ frontend/
        в””в”Җв”Җ .claude/skills/frontend-skill/SKILL.md  # иҮӘеӢ•зҷәиҰӢгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ
```

## 4. Skill гҒ® description (гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјжқЎд»¶) гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№гҒ®гғҷгӮ№гғҲгғ—гғ©гӮҜгғҶгӮЈгӮ№

### йҮҚиҰҒгҒӘгғқгӮӨгғігғҲ

Claude гҒҜ skill гҒ® **description** гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҒиҮӘеӢ•гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ®еҲӨж–ӯгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ

> Claude uses this to decide when to apply the skill.

### гғҷгӮ№гғҲгғ—гғ©гӮҜгғҶгӮЈгӮ№

**1. гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢе®ҹйҡӣгҒ«дҪҝгҒҶиЁҖи‘үгӮ’еҗ«гӮҒгӮӢ**

```yaml
---
description: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
---
```

**2. иӨҮж•°гҒ®гғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгӮ·гғҠгғӘгӮӘгӮ’еҲ—жҢҷ**

```yaml
---
description: Deploy the application to production. Use when you need to deploy, release a new version, or push changes to production.
---
```

**3. е…·дҪ“зҡ„гҒӘгӮӯгғјгғҜгғјгғүгӮ’еҗ«гӮҒгӮӢ**

- гҖҢexplanationгҖҚгҖҢexplainгҖҚгҖҢhow does it workгҖҚ
- гҖҢdeployгҖҚгҖҢreleaseгҖҚгҖҢproductionгҖҚ
- гҖҢreviewгҖҚгҖҢcode reviewгҖҚгҖҢquality checkгҖҚ

**4. жӣ–жҳ§гҒӘиӘ¬жҳҺгҒҜйҒҝгҒ‘гӮӢ**

```yaml
# вқҢ жӮӘгҒ„дҫӢ
description: Helpful skill

# вң… иүҜгҒ„дҫӢ
description: Reviews code for best practices and potential issues. Use when reviewing code, checking PRs, or analyzing code quality.
```

### еҸӮиҖғдҫӢ

е…¬ејҸгғүгӮӯгғҘгғЎгғігғҲгҒ®дҫӢгӮҲгӮҠпјҡ

```yaml
---
name: explain-code
description: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
---
```

```yaml

description: Research a topic thoroughly
context: fork
agent: Explore
---
```

### гғҲгғ©гғ–гғ«гӮ·гғҘгғјгғҶгӮЈгғігӮ°

гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҢгғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ•гӮҢгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲпјҡ

1. **description гҒ«гӮӯгғјгғҜгғјгғүгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҒӢзўәиӘҚ**
2. **гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҢ `/` гғЎгғӢгғҘгғјгҒ«еҮәгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒӢзўәиӘҚ** (`What skills are available?` гҒ§зўәиӘҚ)
3. **гғӘгӮҜгӮЁгӮ№гғҲгӮ’ description гҒ«гғһгғғгғҒгҒ•гҒӣгҒҰгғӘгғ•гғ¬гғјгӮә**
4. **зӣҙжҺҘе‘јгҒіеҮәгҒ—** `/skill-name` гҒ§еј·еҲ¶е®ҹиЎҢ

---

## е®ҹи·өдҫӢпјҡCommand гҒӢгӮү Skill гҒёгҒ®з§»иЎҢ

### з§»иЎҢеүҚ (`.claude/commands/review.md`)

```

### з§»иЎҢеҫҢ (`.claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md`)

Code review instructions...
```

---

# Create Hookify Rule

Create a new hookify rule from conversation analysis or explicit instructions.

## Steps

### If no arguments provided (conversation analysis mode)

1. Use the conversation-analyzer agent via `Task` tool to analyze the current conversation for unwanted behaviors.

2. The agent will return structured findings with suggested rules.

3. For each suggested rule, use AskUserQuestion to confirm with the user:
   - Show the proposed rule (name, event, pattern, action, message)
   - Ask: "Create this rule?" with options: Yes (global), Yes (project), Skip

### If arguments describe a specific rule

1. Load the writing-rules skill with `Skill` for rule format reference.

2. Parse the user's description to determine:
   - Event type (bash, file, stop, prompt, all)
   - Pattern or conditions
   - Action (warn or block)
   - tool_matcher if applicable

3. Draft the rule as a markdown file with YAML frontmatter.

4. Use AskUserQuestion to let the user choose:
   - Rule name (suggest a kebab-case name)
   - Scope: global (`~/.claude/hooks-rules/`) or project (`.claude/hooks-rules/`)
   - Review the draft and confirm

### Writing the rule file

1. Ensure the target directory exists (create if needed).

2. Write the rule file at the chosen location:
   - Global: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/{name}.md`
   - Project: `.claude/hooks-rules/{name}.md`

3. Confirm the rule was created and explain how to test it.

## Rule Template

```markdown
---
name: {name}
enabled: true
event: {event}
action: {action}
{tool_matcher_line}
{pattern_or_conditions}
---

{message}
```

# List Hookify Rules

List all configured hookify rules from both global and project directories.

## Steps

1. Search for rule files in both directories:
   - Global: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`
   - Project: `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`

2. Use Glob to find files matching `*.md` in each directory.

3. Read each file's YAML frontmatter to extract: name, enabled, event, action, tool_matcher, source (global/project).

4. Display results as a table:

```
| Source  | Name              | Event | Action | Enabled | Tool Matcher |
|---------|-------------------|-------|--------|---------|--------------|
| global  | block-rm-rf       | bash  | block  | true    | вҖ”            |
| project | warn-console-log  | file  | warn   | true    | вҖ”            |
| project | block-rm-rf       | bash  | block  | false   | вҖ”            |
```

5. Indicate overrides: if a project rule has the same name as a global rule, note "overrides global" in the table.

6. If no rules are found, suggest creating one with `/hookify:hookify` or by manually creating a file in `~/.claude/hooks-rules/` or `.claude/hooks-rules/`.

---

# Configure Hookify Rules

Interactively enable or disable hookify rules.

## Steps

1. Load the writing-rules skill with `Skill` for reference on rule format.

2. Search for rule files in both directories:
   - Global: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`
   - Project: `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`

3. Read each file to get name, enabled status, event, action, and source.

4. Present rules using AskUserQuestion with multiSelect, letting the user toggle rules on/off:
   - Show each rule with its current status
   - Group by source (global vs project)

5. For each change:
   - If toggling a **project rule**: Edit the `enabled:` field in the frontmatter using Edit.
   - If toggling a **global rule off**: Create a corresponding project rule file in `.claude/hooks-rules/` with the same name but `enabled: false`. This allows disabling global rules per-project without modifying the global file.
   - If toggling a **global rule on** (re-enabling after project override): Delete the project override file or set `enabled: true`.

6. Report changes made.

file.content:
---
allowed-tools:
  - Read
---

# Hookify Help

Read and display the following help text to the user:

## Overview

Hookify lets you create rules that warn or block Claude Code actions. Rules are markdown files with YAML frontmatter stored in `hooks-rules/` directories.

## Rule Locations (priority: low вҶ’ high)

1. **Global**: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`
2. **Project**: `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`

Same-name rules in the project directory override global rules. Set `enabled: false` in a project rule to disable a global rule.

## Commands

- `/hookify:hookify` вҖ” Create a new rule (from conversation analysis or instructions)
- `/hookify:list` вҖ” List all configured rules
- `/hookify:configure` вҖ” Enable/disable rules interactively
- `/hookify:help` вҖ” Show this help

## Quick Start

Create a rule file at `~/.claude/hooks-rules/block-rm-rf.md`:

```markdown
---
name: block-rm-rf
enabled: true
event: bash
pattern: "rm\\s+-rf"
action: block
---

Dangerous rm -rf command detected. Please use a safer alternative.
```

## Rule Format

See the writing-rules skill for complete documentation on rule syntax, events, conditions, and patterns.

## Improvements over upstream hookify

1. **Regex tool_matcher**: Use patterns like `mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).*`
2. **tool_name condition field**: Match on `field: tool_name` in conditions
3. **Directory-based rules**: `hooks-rules/` instead of flat `hookify.*.local.md`
4. **Global + project hierarchy**: CWD-independent global rules with project overrides

     3вҶ’tools:
     4вҶ’  - Read
     5вҶ’  - Grep
     6вҶ’color: "#FF6B6B"
     7вҶ’---
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’Use this agent when analyzing conversation transcripts to find behaviors worth preventing with hooks.
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’<example>
    12вҶ’Context: User is running /hookify command without arguments
    13вҶ’user: "/hookify"
    14вҶ’assistant: "I'll analyze the conversation to find behaviors you want to prevent"
    15вҶ’<commentary>The /hookify command without arguments triggers conversation analysis to find unwanted behaviors.</commentary>
    16вҶ’</example>
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’<example>
    19вҶ’Context: User wants to create hooks from recent frustrations
    20вҶ’user: "Can you look back at this conversation and help me create hooks for the mistakes you made?"
    21вҶ’assistant: "I'll use the conversation-analyzer agent to identify the issues and suggest hooks."
    22вҶ’<commentary>User explicitly asks to analyze conversation for mistakes that should be prevented.</commentary>
    23вҶ’</example>
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’# Conversation Analyzer for Hookify
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’You analyze conversation transcripts to identify unwanted Claude behaviors that should be prevented with hookify rules.
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’## Analysis Process
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’1. **Scan user messages in reverse chronological order** looking for:
    32вҶ’   - Explicit corrections: "Don't do X", "Stop doing Y", "I said not to..."
    33вҶ’   - Frustration signals: "Why did you...", "I didn't ask for...", "Again?!"
    34вҶ’   - Manual reverts or fixes after Claude actions
    35вҶ’   - Repeated issues across multiple turns
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’2. **For each issue found, identify:**
    38вҶ’   - The tool involved (Bash, Edit, Write, etc.)
    39вҶ’   - A regex pattern to catch the behavior
    40вҶ’   - Severity: high (dangerous), medium (style/convention), low (preference)
    41вҶ’   - Whether it should warn or block
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’3. **Output structured findings** for each issue:
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’```
    46вҶ’## Issue: [Brief description]
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’- **Severity**: high | medium | low
    49вҶ’- **Tool**: [tool name or pattern]
    50вҶ’- **Event**: bash | file | stop | prompt | all
    51вҶ’- **Pattern**: [regex pattern]
    52вҶ’- **Action**: warn | block
    53вҶ’- **Context**: [What happened in the conversation]
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’### Suggested Rule
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’---
    58вҶ’name: [kebab-case-name]
    59вҶ’enabled: true
    60вҶ’event: [event]
    61вҶ’action: [action]
    62вҶ’tool_matcher: [tool pattern if needed]
    63вҶ’conditions:
    64вҶ’  - field: [field]
    65вҶ’    operator: regex_match
    66вҶ’    pattern: [pattern]
    67вҶ’---
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’[Warning/blocking message for the user]
    70вҶ’```
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’## Edge Cases
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’- Hypothetical discussions are NOT issues
    75вҶ’- One-time accidents get low severity
    76вҶ’- Subjective preferences are noted but given low priority
    77вҶ’- If no issues found, report "No actionable patterns detected"
    78вҶ’

file.content:
---
model: haiku
tools:
  - Read
  - Grep
color: "#FF6B6B"
---

Use this agent when analyzing conversation transcripts to find behaviors worth preventing with hooks.

<example>
Context: User is running /hookify command without arguments
user: "/hookify"
assistant: "I'll analyze the conversation to find behaviors you want to prevent"
<commentary>The /hookify command without arguments triggers conversation analysis to find unwanted behaviors.</commentary>
</example>

<example>
Context: User wants to create hooks from recent frustrations
user: "Can you look back at this conversation and help me create hooks for the mistakes you made?"
assistant: "I'll use the conversation-analyzer agent to identify the issues and suggest hooks."
<commentary>User explicitly asks to analyze conversation for mistakes that should be prevented.</commentary>
</example>

# Conversation Analyzer for Hookify

You analyze conversation transcripts to identify unwanted Claude behaviors that should be prevented with hookify rules.

## Analysis Process

1. **Scan user messages in reverse chronological order** looking for:
   - Explicit corrections: "Don't do X", "Stop doing Y", "I said not to..."
   - Frustration signals: "Why did you...", "I didn't ask for...", "Again?!"
   - Manual reverts or fixes after Claude actions
   - Repeated issues across multiple turns

2. **For each issue found, identify:**
   - The tool involved (Bash, Edit, Write, etc.)
   - A regex pattern to catch the behavior
   - Severity: high (dangerous), medium (style/convention), low (preference)
   - Whether it should warn or block

3. **Output structured findings** for each issue:

```
## Issue: [Brief description]

- **Severity**: high | medium | low
- **Tool**: [tool name or pattern]
- **Event**: bash | file | stop | prompt | all
- **Pattern**: [regex pattern]
- **Action**: warn | block
- **Context**: [What happened in the conversation]

### Suggested Rule

---
name: [kebab-case-name]
enabled: true
event: [event]
action: [action]
tool_matcher: [tool pattern if needed]
conditions:
  - field: [field]
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: [pattern]
---

[Warning/blocking message for the user]
```

## Edge Cases

- Hypothetical discussions are NOT issues
- One-time accidents get low severity
- Subjective preferences are noted but given low priority
- If no issues found, report "No actionable patterns detected"

file.content:
---
---

This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a hookify rule", "write a hook rule", "configure hookify", "add a hookify rule", or needs guidance on hookify rule syntax and patterns.

# Writing Hookify Rules

Rules are markdown files with YAML frontmatter stored in `hooks-rules/` directories.

## Rule Locations

| Priority | Path | Scope |
|----------|------|-------|
| Low | `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | Global (all projects) |
| High | `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | Project-specific |

Same-name rules: project overrides global. Set `enabled: false` in a project rule to disable a global rule.

## File Format

```markdown
---
name: rule-name
enabled: true
event: bash|file|stop|prompt|all
action: warn|block
pattern: "regex-pattern"
tool_matcher: "regex-pattern"
conditions:
  - field: command|file_path|new_text|content|tool_name
    operator: regex_match|contains|equals|not_contains|starts_with|ends_with
    pattern: "pattern"
---

Message shown when rule triggers.
Use markdown formatting.
```

## Frontmatter Fields

### Required

- **name**: Unique kebab-case identifier. Used for override matching between global/project.
- **enabled**: `true` or `false`.
- **event**: When to evaluate this rule.

### Optional

- **pattern**: Simple regex shorthand. Auto-maps to a condition based on event:
  - `bash` вҶ’ `field: command`
  - `file` вҶ’ `field: new_text`
  - other вҶ’ `field: content`
- **action**: `warn` (default) shows message; `block` denies the operation.
- **tool_matcher**: Regex pattern to match tool names. Examples:
  - `Bash` вҖ” exact match
  - `Edit|Write|MultiEdit` вҖ” OR match
  - `mcp__.*` вҖ” any MCP tool
  - `mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).*` вҖ” MCP tools except Slack
  - `*` вҖ” all tools
- **conditions**: List of conditions (AND logic). Use instead of `pattern` for complex rules.

## Events

| Event | Hook | When |
|-------|------|------|
| `bash` | PreToolUse | Before Bash tool runs |
| `file` | PreToolUse | Before Edit/Write/MultiEdit |
| `stop` | Stop | When Claude wants to stop |
| `prompt` | UserPromptSubmit | When user submits prompt |
| `all` | All hooks | Always evaluated |

## Condition Fields

| Field | Available In | Description |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `command` | bash | Bash command string |
| `file_path` | file | Target file path |
| `new_text` / `new_string` | file | New content being written |
| `old_text` / `old_string` | file (Edit) | Content being replaced |
| `content` | file (Write) | File content |
| `tool_name` | all | Name of the tool being used |
| `user_prompt` | prompt | User's input text |
| `reason` | stop | Stop reason |
| `transcript` | stop | Full conversation transcript |

## Condition Operators

| Operator | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `regex_match` | Regex search (default, case-insensitive) |
| `contains` | Substring match |
| `equals` | Exact match |
| `not_contains` | Inverse substring |
| `starts_with` | Prefix match |
| `ends_with` | Suffix match |

## Examples

### Block dangerous rm

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: true
event: bash
pattern: "rm\\s+-rf\\s+/"
action: block
---

Dangerous rm -rf with absolute path detected. Use a safer alternative.
```

### Warn on console.log in files

```markdown
---
name: warn-console-log
enabled: true
event: file
pattern: "console\\.log\\("
action: warn
---

console.log detected. Consider using a proper logging library.
```

### Block specific MCP tools (regex tool_matcher)

```markdown
---
name: block-slack-mcp
enabled: true
event: all
action: block
tool_matcher: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
conditions:
  - field: tool_name
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
---

Slack MCP tools are blocked in this project.
```

### Require tests before stop

```markdown
---
name: require-tests
enabled: true
event: stop
action: block
conditions:
  - field: transcript
    operator: not_contains
    pattern: "npm test|pytest|cargo test|bun test"
---

No test execution detected. Please run tests before finishing.
```

### Multiple conditions (AND)

```markdown
---
name: warn-env-write
enabled: true
event: file
action: warn
conditions:
  - field: file_path
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "\\.env"
  - field: new_text
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "(password|secret|token|api_key)\\s*="
---

Writing secrets to .env file. Ensure this file is in .gitignore.
```

### Project override to disable global rule

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: false
event: bash
---

(This project-level rule disables the global block-dangerous-rm rule.)
```

## Tips

- Use `pattern` for simple single-condition rules
- Use `conditions` for multi-field or complex matching
- `tool_matcher` filters BEFORE conditions are checked (performance optimization)
- Test regex patterns with: `echo "test string" | python3 -c "import re,sys; print(bool(re.search(r'pattern', sys.stdin.read())))"`
- Start with `action: warn` and switch to `block` once confident

гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгҖҢhookify гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҖҚгҖҢгғ•гғғгӮҜгғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’иҝҪеҠ гҖҚгҖҢhookifyгҖҚгҖҢгғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’дҪңгҒЈгҒҰгҖҚгҖҢгҒ“гҒ®иЎҢеӢ•гӮ’йҳІжӯўгҒ—гҒҰгҖҚгҖҢhook гӮ’иЁӯе®ҡгҖҚгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®гғӘгӮҜгӮЁгӮ№гғҲгҖҒгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜдјҡи©ұеҶ…гҒ®е•ҸйЎҢиЎҢеӢ•гӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒ—гҒҰгғ«гғјгғ«еҢ–гҒҷгӮӢйҡӣгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

# гғ«гғјгғ«дҪңжҲҗ

hookify гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’ж–°иҰҸдҪңжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮдјҡи©ұеҲҶжһҗгғўгғјгғүгҒЁжҳҺзӨәзҡ„жҢҮзӨәгғўгғјгғүгҒ® 2 гҒӨгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҖӮ

## еј•ж•°гҒӘгҒ— (дјҡи©ұеҲҶжһҗгғўгғјгғү)

1. conversation-analyzer гӮЁгғјгӮёгӮ§гғігғҲгӮ’ `Task` гғ„гғјгғ«гҒ§иө·еӢ•гҒ—гҖҒзҸҫеңЁгҒ®дјҡи©ұгҒӢгӮүйҳІжӯўгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚиЎҢеӢ•гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

2. гӮЁгғјгӮёгӮ§гғігғҲгҒҢж§ӢйҖ еҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹзөҗжһңгӮ’иҝ”гҒҷгҖӮ

3. жҸҗжЎҲгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ”гҒЁгҒ« AskUserQuestion гҒ§зўәиӘҚгҒҷгӮӢ:
   - гғ«гғјгғ«еҶ…е®№ (name, event, pattern, action, message) гӮ’иЎЁзӨә
   - гҖҢгҒ“гҒ®гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҒӢ?гҖҚвҶ’ йҒёжҠһиӮў: гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гҒ«дҪңжҲҗ / гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ«дҪңжҲҗ / гӮ№гӮӯгғғгғ—

## еј•ж•°гҒӮгӮҠ (жҳҺзӨәзҡ„жҢҮзӨәгғўгғјгғү)

1. writing-rules гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гӮ’ `Skill` гғ„гғјгғ«гҒ§иӘӯгҒҝиҫјгҒҝгҖҒгғ«гғјгғ«еҪўејҸгӮ’еҸӮз…§гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

2. гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒ®иӘ¬жҳҺгҒӢгӮүд»ҘдёӢгӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢ:
   - гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲгӮҝгӮӨгғ— (bash, file, stop, prompt, all)
   - гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ conditions
   - гӮўгӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі (warn гҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ block)
   - еҝ…иҰҒгҒ«еҝңгҒҳгҒҰ tool_matcher

3. YAML frontmatter д»ҳгҒҚгҒ®гғһгғјгӮҜгғҖгӮҰгғігғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’дёӢжӣёгҒҚгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

4. AskUserQuestion гҒ§гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒ«зўәиӘҚгҒҷгӮӢ:
   - гғ«гғјгғ«еҗҚ (kebab-case гӮ’жҸҗжЎҲ)
   - гӮ№гӮігғјгғ—: гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ« (`~/.claude/hooks-rules/`) гҒҫгҒҹгҒҜгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ (`.claude/hooks-rules/`)
   - дёӢжӣёгҒҚгҒ®зўәиӘҚ

## гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ®жӣёгҒҚиҫјгҒҝ

1. еҜҫиұЎгғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒҢеӯҳеңЁгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜдҪңжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

# гғ«гғјгғ«дёҖиҰ§иЎЁзӨә

## жүӢй Ҷ

1. д»ҘдёӢгҒ® 2 гҒӨгҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒӢгӮүгғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’жӨңзҙўгҒҷгӮӢ:
   - гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`
   - гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ: `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`

2. Glob гҒ§еҗ„гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ® `*.md` гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’жӨңзҙўгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

3. еҗ„гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ® YAML frontmatter гӮ’ Read гҒ§иӘӯгҒҝеҸ–гӮҠгҖҒд»ҘдёӢгӮ’жҠҪеҮәгҒҷгӮӢ: name, enabled, event, action, tool_matcher, гӮҪгғјгӮ№ (global/project)гҖӮ

4. гғҶгғјгғ–гғ«еҪўејҸгҒ§иЎЁзӨәгҒҷгӮӢ:

```
| гӮҪгғјгӮ№  | еҗҚеүҚ              | гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ | гӮўгӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі | жңүеҠ№ | Tool Matcher |
|---------|-------------------|----------|-----------|------|--------------|
| global  | block-rm-rf       | bash     | block     | true | вҖ”            |
| project | warn-console-log  | file     | warn      | true | вҖ”            |
| project | block-rm-rf       | bash     | block     | false| вҖ”            |
```

5. гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гҒҢгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гҒЁеҗҢеҗҚгҒ®е ҙеҗҲгҖҒгҖҢгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гӮ’дёҠжӣёгҒҚгҖҚгҒЁжіЁиЁҳгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

6. гғ«гғјгғ«гҒҢиҰӢгҒӨгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜгҖҒ`/hookify:hookify` гҒ§гҒ®гғ«гғјгғ«дҪңжҲҗгҖҒгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ `~/.claude/hooks-rules/` гӮ„ `.claude/hooks-rules/` гҒёгҒ®жүӢеӢ•дҪңжҲҗгӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

4. AskUserQuestion (multiSelect) гҒ§гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®гғҲгӮ°гғ«гӮ’гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒ«жҸҗзӨәгҒҷгӮӢ:

   - гӮҪгғјгӮ№ (global / project) гҒ§гӮ°гғ«гғјгғ—еҢ–

5. еӨүжӣҙгҒ”гҒЁгҒ®еҮҰзҗҶ:
   - **гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®еҲҮгӮҠжӣҝгҒҲ**: Edit гҒ§гғ•гғӯгғігғҲгғһгӮҝгғјгҒ® `enabled:` гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғүгӮ’еӨүжӣҙгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ
   - **гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–**: `.claude/hooks-rules/` гҒ«еҗҢеҗҚгҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’ `enabled: false` гҒ§дҪңжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒҜеӨүжӣҙгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮ
   - **гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®еҶҚжңүеҠ№еҢ–** (гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲдёҠжӣёгҒҚгҒ®и§ЈйҷӨ): гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲдёҠжӣёгҒҚгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’еүҠйҷӨгҒҷгӮӢгҒӢгҖҒ`enabled: true` гҒ«иЁӯе®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

6. еӨүжӣҙеҶ…е®№гӮ’е ұе‘ҠгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

# гғ«гғјгғ«иЁӯе®ҡ

hookify гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®жңүеҠ№/з„ЎеҠ№гӮ’гӮӨгғігӮҝгғ©гӮҜгғҶгӮЈгғ–гҒ«еҲҮгӮҠжӣҝгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ

## жүӢй Ҷ

1. writing-rules гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гӮ’ `Skill` гғ„гғјгғ«гҒ§иӘӯгҒҝиҫјгҒҝгҖҒгғ«гғјгғ«еҪўејҸгӮ’еҸӮз…§гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

2. д»ҘдёӢгҒ® 2 гҒӨгҒ®гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒӢгӮүгғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’жӨңзҙўгҒҷгӮӢ:
   - гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`
   - гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ: `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`

3. еҗ„гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’ Read гҒ§иӘӯгҒҝеҸ–гӮҠгҖҒname, enabled, event, action, гӮҪгғјгӮ№гӮ’еҸ–еҫ—гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

4. AskUserQuestion (multiSelect) гҒ§гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®гғҲгӮ°гғ«гӮ’гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒ«жҸҗзӨәгҒҷгӮӢ:
   - еҗ„гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®зҸҫеңЁгҒ®гӮ№гғҶгғјгӮҝгӮ№гӮ’иЎЁзӨә
   - гӮҪгғјгӮ№ (global / project) гҒ§гӮ°гғ«гғјгғ—еҢ–

5. еӨүжӣҙгҒ”гҒЁгҒ®еҮҰзҗҶ:
   - **гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®еҲҮгӮҠжӣҝгҒҲ**: Edit гҒ§гғ•гғӯгғігғҲгғһгӮҝгғјгҒ® `enabled:` гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғүгӮ’еӨүжӣҙгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ
   - **гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–**: `.claude/hooks-rules/` гҒ«еҗҢеҗҚгҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’ `enabled: false` гҒ§дҪңжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒҜеӨүжӣҙгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„гҖӮ
   - **гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®еҶҚжңүеҠ№еҢ–** (гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲдёҠжӣёгҒҚгҒ®и§ЈйҷӨ): гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲдёҠжӣёгҒҚгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’еүҠйҷӨгҒҷгӮӢгҒӢгҖҒ`enabled: true` гҒ«иЁӯе®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгҖҢhookify гғҳгғ«гғ—гҖҚгҖҢhookify гҒ®дҪҝгҒ„ж–№гҖҚгҖҢhookify helpгҖҚгҖҢгғ•гғғгӮҜгҒ®иЁӯе®ҡж–№жі•гҖҚгҖҢhookify гҒЁгҒҜгҖҚгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®гғӘгӮҜгӮЁгӮ№гғҲгҒ§дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

# Hookify гғҳгғ«гғ—

д»ҘдёӢгҒ®гғҳгғ«гғ—гғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲгӮ’гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒ«иЎЁзӨәгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

## жҰӮиҰҒ

Hookify гҒҜ Claude Code гҒ®гӮўгӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғігҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰиӯҰе‘ҠгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜгғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгҒҷгӮӢгғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгғ—гғ©гӮ°гӮӨгғігҖӮгғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜ YAML гғ•гғӯгғігғҲгғһгӮҝгғјд»ҳгҒҚгҒ®гғһгғјгӮҜгғҖгӮҰгғігғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒЁгҒ—гҒҰ `hooks-rules/` гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ«ж јзҙҚгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

## гғ«гғјгғ«й…ҚзҪ®е ҙжүҖ (е„Әе…Ҳй ҶдҪҚ: дҪҺ вҶ’ й«ҳ)

1. **гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«**: `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`
2. **гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ**: `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md`

еҗҢеҗҚгғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеҒҙгҒҢе„Әе…Ҳ (дёҠжӣёгҒҚ)гҖӮгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ§ `enabled: false` гӮ’иЁӯе®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–гҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҖӮ

## гӮ№гӮӯгғ«

- `/hookify:hookify` вҖ” ж–°иҰҸгғ«гғјгғ«дҪңжҲҗ (дјҡи©ұеҲҶжһҗгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜжҳҺзӨәзҡ„жҢҮзӨәгҒӢгӮү)
- `/hookify:list` вҖ” иЁӯе®ҡжёҲгҒҝгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®дёҖиҰ§иЎЁзӨә
- `/hookify:configure` вҖ” гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®жңүеҠ№/з„ЎеҠ№гӮ’гӮӨгғігӮҝгғ©гӮҜгғҶгӮЈгғ–гҒ«еҲҮгӮҠжӣҝгҒҲ
- `/hookify:help` вҖ” гҒ“гҒ®гғҳгғ«гғ—гӮ’иЎЁзӨә

## гӮҜгӮӨгғғгӮҜгӮ№гӮҝгғјгғҲ

`~/.claude/hooks-rules/block-rm-rf.md` гҒ«гғ«гғјгғ«гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҒҷгӮӢ:

```markdown
---
name: block-rm-rf
enabled: true
event: bash
pattern: "rm\\s+-rf"
action: block
---

еҚұйҷәгҒӘ rm -rf гӮігғһгғігғүгӮ’жӨңеҮәгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮе®үе…ЁгҒӘд»ЈжӣҝжүӢж®өгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

## гғ«гғјгғ«еҪўејҸ

гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®ж§Ӣж–ҮгҖҒгӮӨгғҷгғігғҲгҖҒжқЎд»¶гҖҒгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒ®и©ізҙ°гҒҜ writing-rules гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гӮ’еҸӮз…§гҖӮ

## е…¬ејҸ hookify гҒӢгӮүгҒ®ж”№е–„зӮ№

1. **regex tool_matcher**: `mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).*` гҒ®гӮҲгҒҶгҒӘгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒҢдҪҝгҒҲгӮӢ
2. **tool_name condition гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү**: `field: tool_name` гҒ§гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢжқЎд»¶гҒҢжӣёгҒ‘гӮӢ
3. **гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгғҷгғјгӮ№гҒ®гғ«гғјгғ«з®ЎзҗҶ**: `hookify.*.local.md` гҒ®гғ•гғ©гғғгғҲз®ЎзҗҶгҒ§гҒҜгҒӘгҒҸ `hooks-rules/` гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ«ж•ҙзҗҶ
4. **гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ« + гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ® 2 йҡҺеұӨ**: CWD гҒ«дҫқеӯҳгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„зўәе®ҹгҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«иӘӯгҒҝиҫјгҒҝ

гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгҖҢhookify гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҖҚгҖҢгғ•гғғгӮҜгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№гҖҚгҖҢhookify гғ«гғјгғ«ж§Ӣж–ҮгҖҚгҖҢгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®еҪўејҸгӮ’ж•ҷгҒҲгҒҰгҖҚгҖҢжқЎд»¶гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№гҖҚгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®гғӘгӮҜгӮЁгӮ№гғҲгҖҒгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ hookify гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®ж§Ӣж–ҮгӮ„гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒ®гӮ¬гӮӨгғҖгғігӮ№гҒҢеҝ…иҰҒгҒӘйҡӣгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

# Hookify гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№

гғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜ YAML гғ•гғӯгғігғҲгғһгӮҝгғјд»ҳгҒҚгҒ®гғһгғјгӮҜгғҖгӮҰгғігғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ§гҖҒ`hooks-rules/` гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ«ж јзҙҚгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

## гғ«гғјгғ«й…ҚзҪ®е ҙжүҖ

| е„Әе…ҲеәҰ | гғ‘гӮ№ | гӮ№гӮігғјгғ— |
|--------|------|----------|
| дҪҺ | `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ« (е…Ёгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ) |
| й«ҳ | `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеӣәжңү |

еҗҢеҗҚгғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеҒҙгҒҢдёҠжӣёгҒҚгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ§ `enabled: false` гҒ«гҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–гҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҖӮ

## гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҪўејҸ

```markdown
---
name: rule-name
enabled: true
event: bash|file|stop|prompt|all
action: warn|block
pattern: "regex-pattern"
tool_matcher: "regex-pattern"
conditions:
  - field: command|file_path|new_text|content|tool_name
    operator: regex_match|contains|equals|not_contains|starts_with|ends_with
    pattern: "pattern"
---

гғ«гғјгғ«гҒҢгғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹжҷӮгҒ«иЎЁзӨәгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёгҖӮ
гғһгғјгӮҜгғҖгӮҰгғіеҪўејҸгҒ§иЁҳиҝ°еҸҜиғҪгҖӮ
```

## гғ•гғӯгғігғҲгғһгӮҝгғјгғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү

### еҝ…й Ҳ

- **name**: дёҖж„ҸгҒ® kebab-case иӯҳеҲҘеӯҗгҖӮгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«/гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲй–“гҒ®дёҠжӣёгҒҚгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°гҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҖӮ
- **enabled**: `true` гҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ `false`гҖӮ
- **event**: гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’и©•дҫЎгҒҷгӮӢгӮҝгӮӨгғҹгғігӮ°гҖӮ

### д»»ж„Ҹ

- **pattern**: з°Ўжҳ“ regexгҖӮгӮӨгғҷгғігғҲгҒ«еҝңгҒҳгҒҰиҮӘеӢ•зҡ„гҒ« condition гҒ«гғһгғғгғ”гғігӮ°:
  - `bash` вҶ’ `field: command`
  - `file` вҶ’ `field: new_text`
  - гҒқгҒ®д»– вҶ’ `field: content`
- **action**: `warn` (гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲ) гҒҜгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёиЎЁзӨәгҖҒ`block` гҒҜж“ҚдҪңгӮ’жӢ’еҗҰгҖӮ
- **tool_matcher**: гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒ«гғһгғғгғҒгҒҷгӮӢ regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҖӮдҫӢ:
  - `Bash` вҖ” е®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙ
  - `Edit|Write|MultiEdit` вҖ” OR гғһгғғгғҒ
  - `mcp__.*` вҖ” е…Ё MCP гғ„гғјгғ«
  - `mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).*` вҖ” Slack д»ҘеӨ–гҒ® MCP гғ„гғјгғ«
  - `*` вҖ” е…Ёгғ„гғјгғ«
- **conditions**: жқЎд»¶гҒ®гғӘгӮ№гғҲ (AND и«–зҗҶ)гҖӮиӨҮйӣ‘гҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ«гҒҜ `pattern` гҒ®д»ЈгӮҸгӮҠгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҖӮ

## гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ

| гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ | гғ•гғғгӮҜ | гӮҝгӮӨгғҹгғігӮ° |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| `bash` | PreToolUse | Bash гғ„гғјгғ«е®ҹиЎҢеүҚ |
| `file` | PreToolUse | Edit/Write/MultiEdit е®ҹиЎҢеүҚ |
| `stop` | Stop | Claude гҒҢеҒңжӯўгҒ—гӮҲгҒҶгҒЁгҒ—гҒҹжҷӮ |
| `prompt` | UserPromptSubmit | гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢгғ—гғӯгғігғ—гғҲгӮ’йҖҒдҝЎгҒ—гҒҹжҷӮ |
| `all` | е…Ёгғ•гғғгӮҜ | еёёгҒ«и©•дҫЎ |

## жқЎд»¶гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү

| гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү | еҲ©з”ЁеҸҜиғҪгӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ | иӘ¬жҳҺ |
|-----------|-----------------|------|
| `command` | bash | Bash гӮігғһгғігғүж–Үеӯ—еҲ— |
| `file_path` | file | еҜҫиұЎгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гғ‘гӮ№ |
| `new_text` / `new_string` | file | жӣёгҒҚиҫјгҒҫгӮҢгӮӢж–°гҒ—гҒ„еҶ…е®№ |
| `old_text` / `old_string` | file (Edit) | зҪ®жҸӣгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢеҶ…е®№ |
| `content` | file (Write) | гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҶ…е®№ |
| `tool_name` | all | дҪҝз”ЁгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚ |
| `user_prompt` | prompt | гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒ®е…ҘеҠӣгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲ |
| `reason` | stop | еҒңжӯўзҗҶз”ұ |
| `transcript` | stop | дјҡи©ұе…Ёж–Ү |

## жқЎд»¶жј”з®—еӯҗ

| жј”з®—еӯҗ | иӘ¬жҳҺ |
|--------|------|
| `regex_match` | regex жӨңзҙў (гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгҖҒеӨ§ж–Үеӯ—е°Ҹж–Үеӯ—гӮ’еҢәеҲҘгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„) |
| `contains` | йғЁеҲҶж–Үеӯ—еҲ—гғһгғғгғҒ |
| `equals` | е®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙ |
| `not_contains` | йғЁеҲҶж–Үеӯ—еҲ—гҒ®еҗҰе®ҡ |
| `starts_with` | еүҚж–№дёҖиҮҙ |
| `ends_with` | еҫҢж–№дёҖиҮҙ |

## гғ«гғјгғ«дҫӢ

### еҚұйҷәгҒӘ rm гӮ’гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜ

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: true
event: bash
pattern: "rm\\s+-rf\\s+/"
action: block
---

зө¶еҜҫгғ‘гӮ№гҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢ rm -rf гӮ’жӨңеҮәгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮе®үе…ЁгҒӘд»ЈжӣҝжүӢж®өгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҶ…гҒ® console.log гҒ«иӯҰе‘Ҡ

```markdown
---
name: warn-console-log
enabled: true
event: file
pattern: "console\\.log\\("
action: warn
---

console.log гӮ’жӨңеҮәгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮйҒ©еҲҮгҒӘгғӯгӮ®гғігӮ°гғ©гӮӨгғ–гғ©гғӘгҒ®дҪҝз”ЁгӮ’жӨңиЁҺгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### зү№е®ҡгҒ® MCP гғ„гғјгғ«гӮ’гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜ (regex tool_matcher)

```markdown
---
name: block-slack-mcp
enabled: true
event: all
action: block
tool_matcher: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
conditions:
  - field: tool_name
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
---

Slack MCP гғ„гғјгғ«гҒҜгҒ“гҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ§гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ
```

### еҒңжӯўеүҚгҒ«гғҶгӮ№гғҲе®ҹиЎҢгӮ’иҰҒжұӮ

```markdown
---
name: require-tests
enabled: true
event: stop
action: block
conditions:
  - field: transcript
    operator: not_contains
    pattern: "npm test|pytest|cargo test|bun test"
---

гғҶгӮ№гғҲе®ҹиЎҢгҒҢжӨңеҮәгҒ•гӮҢгҒҫгҒӣгӮ“гҒ§гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮе®ҢдәҶеүҚгҒ«гғҶгӮ№гғҲгӮ’е®ҹиЎҢгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### иӨҮж•°жқЎд»¶ (AND)

```markdown
---
name: warn-env-write
enabled: true
event: file
action: warn
conditions:
  - field: file_path
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "\\.env"
  - field: new_text
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "(password|secret|token|api_key)\\s*="
---

.env гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ«гӮ·гғјгӮҜгғ¬гғғгғҲгӮ’жӣёгҒҚиҫјгӮ“гҒ§гҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒҢ .gitignore гҒ«еҗ«гҒҫгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ§з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: false
event: bash
---

(гҒ“гҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®гғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гҒ® block-dangerous-rm гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ)
```

## Tips

- еҚҳдёҖжқЎд»¶гҒ®гӮ·гғігғ—гғ«гҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ«гҒҜ `pattern` гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢ
- иӨҮж•°гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғүгӮ„иӨҮйӣ‘гҒӘгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°гҒ«гҒҜ `conditions` гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢ
- `tool_matcher` гҒҜ conditions гҒ®еүҚгҒ«гғ•гӮЈгғ«гӮҝгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ (гғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№жңҖйҒ©еҢ–)
- regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒ®гғҶгӮ№гғҲ: `echo "test string" | python3 -c "import re,sys; print(bool(re.search(r'pattern', sys.stdin.read())))"`
- гҒҫгҒҡ `action: warn` гҒ§и©ҰгҒ—гҖҒзўәдҝЎгҒҢжҢҒгҒҰгҒҹгӮү `block` гҒ«еҲҮгӮҠжӣҝгҒҲгӮӢ

    54вҶ’- **tool_matcher**: гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒ«гғһгғғгғҒгҒҷгӮӢ regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҖӮдҫӢ:
    55вҶ’  - `Bash` вҖ” е®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙ

   160вҶ’```

   196вҶ’- еҚҳдёҖжқЎд»¶гҒ®гӮ·гғігғ—гғ«гҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ«гҒҜ `pattern` гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢ

гҒ“гҒ®гӮ№гӮӯгғ«гҒҜгҖҒгҖҢhookify гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’дҪңжҲҗгҖҚгҖҢгғ•гғғгӮҜгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№гҖҚгҖҢhookify гғ«гғјгғ«ж§Ӣж–ҮгҖҚгҖҢгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®еҪўејҸгӮ’ж•ҷгҒҲгҒҰгҖҚгҖҢжқЎд»¶гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№гҖҚгҒӘгҒ©гҒ®гғӘгӮҜгӮЁгӮ№гғҲгҖҒгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ hookify гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®ж§Ӣж–ҮгӮ„гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒ®гӮ¬гӮӨгғҖгғігӮ№гҒҢеҝ…иҰҒгҒӘйҡӣгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

# Hookify гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ®жӣёгҒҚж–№

гғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜ YAML гғ•гғӯгғігғҲгғһгӮҝгғјд»ҳгҒҚгҒ®гғһгғјгӮҜгғҖгӮҰгғігғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ§гҖҒ`hooks-rules/` гғҮгӮЈгғ¬гӮҜгғҲгғӘгҒ«ж јзҙҚгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

## гғ«гғјгғ«й…ҚзҪ®е ҙжүҖ

| е„Әе…ҲеәҰ | гғ‘гӮ№ | гӮ№гӮігғјгғ— |
|--------|------|----------|
| дҪҺ | `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ« (е…Ёгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲ) |
| й«ҳ | `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеӣәжңү |

еҗҢеҗҚгғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲеҒҙгҒҢдёҠжӣёгҒҚгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮгғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ§ `enabled: false` гҒ«гҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–гҒ§гҒҚгӮӢгҖӮ

## гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҪўејҸ

```markdown
---
name: rule-name
enabled: true
event: bash|file|stop|prompt|all
action: warn|block
pattern: "regex-pattern"
tool_matcher: "regex-pattern"
conditions:
  - field: command|file_path|new_text|content|tool_name
    operator: regex_match|contains|equals|not_contains|starts_with|ends_with
    pattern: "pattern"
---

гғ«гғјгғ«гҒҢгғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹжҷӮгҒ«иЎЁзӨәгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёгҖӮ
гғһгғјгӮҜгғҖгӮҰгғіеҪўејҸгҒ§иЁҳиҝ°еҸҜиғҪгҖӮ
```

## гғ•гғӯгғігғҲгғһгӮҝгғјгғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү

### еҝ…й Ҳ

- **name**: дёҖж„ҸгҒ® kebab-case иӯҳеҲҘеӯҗгҖӮгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«/гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲй–“гҒ®дёҠжӣёгҒҚгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°гҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҖӮ
- **enabled**: `true` гҒҫгҒҹгҒҜ `false`гҖӮ
- **event**: гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’и©•дҫЎгҒҷгӮӢгӮҝгӮӨгғҹгғігӮ°гҖӮ

### д»»ж„Ҹ

- **pattern**: з°Ўжҳ“ regexгҖӮгӮӨгғҷгғігғҲгҒ«еҝңгҒҳгҒҰиҮӘеӢ•зҡ„гҒ« condition гҒ«гғһгғғгғ”гғігӮ°:
  - `bash` вҶ’ `field: command`
  - `file` вҶ’ `field: new_text`
  - гҒқгҒ®д»– вҶ’ `field: content`
- **action**: `warn` (гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲ) гҒҜгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёиЎЁзӨәгҖҒ`block` гҒҜж“ҚдҪңгӮ’жӢ’еҗҰгҖӮ
- **tool_matcher**: гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒ«гғһгғғгғҒгҒҷгӮӢ regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҖӮдҫӢ:
  - `Bash` вҖ” е®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙ
  - `Edit|Write|MultiEdit` вҖ” OR гғһгғғгғҒ
  - `mcp__.*` вҖ” е…Ё MCP гғ„гғјгғ«
  - `mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).*` вҖ” Slack д»ҘеӨ–гҒ® MCP гғ„гғјгғ«
  - `*` вҖ” е…Ёгғ„гғјгғ«
- **conditions**: жқЎд»¶гҒ®гғӘгӮ№гғҲ (AND и«–зҗҶ)гҖӮиӨҮйӣ‘гҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ«гҒҜ `pattern` гҒ®д»ЈгӮҸгӮҠгҒ«дҪҝз”ЁгҖӮ

## гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ

| гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ | гғ•гғғгӮҜ | гӮҝгӮӨгғҹгғігӮ° |

| `stop` | Stop | Claude гҒҢеҒңжӯўгҒ—гӮҲгҒҶгҒЁгҒ—гҒҹжҷӮ |
| `prompt` | UserPromptSubmit | гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢгғ—гғӯгғігғ—гғҲгӮ’йҖҒдҝЎгҒ—гҒҹжҷӮ |
| `all` | е…Ёгғ•гғғгӮҜ | еёёгҒ«и©•дҫЎ |

## жқЎд»¶гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү

| гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғү | еҲ©з”ЁеҸҜиғҪгӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ | иӘ¬жҳҺ |
|-----------|-----------------|------|
| `command` | bash | Bash гӮігғһгғігғүж–Үеӯ—еҲ— |
| `file_path` | file | еҜҫиұЎгғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гғ‘гӮ№ |
| `new_text` / `new_string` | file | жӣёгҒҚиҫјгҒҫгӮҢгӮӢж–°гҒ—гҒ„еҶ…е®№ |
| `old_text` / `old_string` | file (Edit) | зҪ®жҸӣгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢеҶ…е®№ |
| `content` | file (Write) | гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҶ…е®№ |
| `tool_name` | all | дҪҝз”ЁгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚ |
| `user_prompt` | prompt | гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒ®е…ҘеҠӣгғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲ |
| `reason` | stop | еҒңжӯўзҗҶз”ұ |
| `transcript` | stop | дјҡи©ұе…Ёж–Ү |

## жқЎд»¶жј”з®—еӯҗ

| жј”з®—еӯҗ | иӘ¬жҳҺ |
|--------|------|
| `regex_match` | regex жӨңзҙў (гғҮгғ•гӮ©гғ«гғҲгҖҒеӨ§ж–Үеӯ—е°Ҹж–Үеӯ—гӮ’еҢәеҲҘгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„) |
| `contains` | йғЁеҲҶж–Үеӯ—еҲ—гғһгғғгғҒ |
| `equals` | е®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙ |
| `not_contains` | йғЁеҲҶж–Үеӯ—еҲ—гҒ®еҗҰе®ҡ |
| `starts_with` | еүҚж–№дёҖиҮҙ |
| `ends_with` | еҫҢж–№дёҖиҮҙ |

## гғ«гғјгғ«дҫӢ

### еҚұйҷәгҒӘ rm гӮ’гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜ

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: true
event: bash
pattern: "rm\\s+-rf\\s+/"
action: block
---

зө¶еҜҫгғ‘гӮ№гҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢ rm -rf гӮ’жӨңеҮәгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮе®үе…ЁгҒӘд»ЈжӣҝжүӢж®өгӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«еҶ…гҒ® console.log гҒ«иӯҰе‘Ҡ

```markdown
---
name: warn-console-log
enabled: true
event: file
pattern: "console\\.log\\("
action: warn
---

console.log гӮ’жӨңеҮәгҒ—гҒҫгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮйҒ©еҲҮгҒӘгғӯгӮ®гғігӮ°гғ©гӮӨгғ–гғ©гғӘгҒ®дҪҝз”ЁгӮ’жӨңиЁҺгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### зү№е®ҡгҒ® MCP гғ„гғјгғ«гӮ’гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜ (regex tool_matcher)

```markdown
---
name: block-slack-mcp
enabled: true
event: all
action: block
tool_matcher: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
conditions:
  - field: tool_name
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
---

Slack MCP гғ„гғјгғ«гҒҜгҒ“гҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ§гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ
```

### еҒңжӯўеүҚгҒ«гғҶгӮ№гғҲе®ҹиЎҢгӮ’иҰҒжұӮ

```markdown
---
name: require-tests
enabled: true
event: stop
action: block
conditions:
  - field: transcript
    operator: not_contains
    pattern: "npm test|pytest|cargo test|bun test"
---

гғҶгӮ№гғҲе®ҹиЎҢгҒҢжӨңеҮәгҒ•гӮҢгҒҫгҒӣгӮ“гҒ§гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮе®ҢдәҶеүҚгҒ«гғҶгӮ№гғҲгӮ’е®ҹиЎҢгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### иӨҮж•°жқЎд»¶ (AND)

```markdown
---
name: warn-env-write
enabled: true
event: file
action: warn
conditions:
  - field: file_path
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "\\.env"
  - field: new_text
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "(password|secret|token|api_key)\\s*="
---

.env гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒ«гӮ·гғјгӮҜгғ¬гғғгғҲгӮ’жӣёгҒҚиҫјгӮ“гҒ§гҒ„гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®гғ•гӮЎгӮӨгғ«гҒҢ .gitignore гҒ«еҗ«гҒҫгӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’зўәиӘҚгҒ—гҒҰгҒҸгҒ гҒ•гҒ„гҖӮ
```

### гӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгҒ§з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: false
event: bash
---

(гҒ“гҒ®гғ—гғӯгӮёгӮ§гӮҜгғҲгғ¬гғҷгғ«гҒ®гғ«гғјгғ«гҒҜгӮ°гғӯгғјгғҗгғ«гҒ® block-dangerous-rm гғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’з„ЎеҠ№еҢ–гҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ)
```

## Tips

- еҚҳдёҖжқЎд»¶гҒ®гӮ·гғігғ—гғ«гҒӘгғ«гғјгғ«гҒ«гҒҜ `pattern` гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢ
- иӨҮж•°гғ•гӮЈгғјгғ«гғүгӮ„иӨҮйӣ‘гҒӘгғһгғғгғҒгғігӮ°гҒ«гҒҜ `conditions` гӮ’дҪҝз”ЁгҒҷгӮӢ
- `tool_matcher` гҒҜ conditions гҒ®еүҚгҒ«гғ•гӮЈгғ«гӮҝгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢ (гғ‘гғ•гӮ©гғјгғһгғігӮ№жңҖйҒ©еҢ–)
- regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒ®гғҶгӮ№гғҲ: `echo "test string" | python3 -c "import re,sys; print(bool(re.search(r'pattern', sys.stdin.read())))"`
- гҒҫгҒҡ `action: warn` гҒ§и©ҰгҒ—гҖҒзўәдҝЎгҒҢжҢҒгҒҰгҒҹгӮү `block` гҒ«еҲҮгӮҠжӣҝгҒҲгӮӢ

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
---

This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a hookify rule", "write a hook rule", "configure hookify", "add a hookify rule", or needs guidance on hookify rule syntax and patterns.

# Writing Hookify Rules

Rules are markdown files with YAML frontmatter stored in `hooks-rules/` directories.

## Rule Locations

| Priority | Path | Scope |
|----------|------|-------|
| Low | `~/.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | Global (all projects) |
| High | `.claude/hooks-rules/*.md` | Project-specific |

Same-name rules: project overrides global. Set `enabled: false` in a project rule to disable a global rule.

## File Format

```markdown
---
name: rule-name
enabled: true
event: bash|file|stop|prompt|all
action: warn|block
pattern: "regex-pattern"
tool_matcher: "regex-pattern"
conditions:
  - field: command|file_path|new_text|content|tool_name
    operator: regex_match|contains|equals|not_contains|starts_with|ends_with
    pattern: "pattern"
---

Message shown when rule triggers.
Use markdown formatting.
```

## Frontmatter Fields

### Required

- **name**: Unique kebab-case identifier. Used for override matching between global/project.
- **enabled**: `true` or `false`.
- **event**: When to evaluate this rule.

### Optional

- **pattern**: Simple regex shorthand. Auto-maps to a condition based on event:
  - `bash` вҶ’ `field: command`
  - `file` вҶ’ `field: new_text`
  - other вҶ’ `field: content`
- **action**: `warn` (default) shows message; `block` denies the operation.
- **tool_matcher**: Regex pattern to match tool names. Examples:
  - `Bash` вҖ” exact match
  - `Edit|Write|MultiEdit` вҖ” OR match
  - `mcp__.*` вҖ” any MCP tool
  - `mcp__claude_ai_(?!Slack__).*` вҖ” MCP tools except Slack
  - `*` вҖ” all tools
- **conditions**: List of conditions (AND logic). Use instead of `pattern` for complex rules.

## Events

| Event | Hook | When |
|-------|------|------|
| `bash` | PreToolUse | Before Bash tool runs |
| `file` | PreToolUse | Before Edit/Write/MultiEdit |
| `stop` | Stop | When Claude wants to stop |
| `prompt` | UserPromptSubmit | When user submits prompt |
| `all` | All hooks | Always evaluated |

## Condition Fields

| Field | Available In | Description |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `command` | bash | Bash command string |
| `file_path` | file | Target file path |
| `new_text` / `new_string` | file | New content being written |
| `old_text` / `old_string` | file (Edit) | Content being replaced |
| `content` | file (Write) | File content |
| `tool_name` | all | Name of the tool being used |
| `user_prompt` | prompt | User's input text |
| `reason` | stop | Stop reason |
| `transcript` | stop | Full conversation transcript |

## Condition Operators

| Operator | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `regex_match` | Regex search (default, case-insensitive) |
| `contains` | Substring match |
| `equals` | Exact match |
| `not_contains` | Inverse substring |
| `starts_with` | Prefix match |
| `ends_with` | Suffix match |

## Examples

### Block dangerous rm

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: true
event: bash
pattern: "rm\\s+-rf\\s+/"
action: block
---

Dangerous rm -rf with absolute path detected. Use a safer alternative.
```

### Warn on console.log in files

```markdown
---
name: warn-console-log
enabled: true
event: file
pattern: "console\\.log\\("
action: warn
---

console.log detected. Consider using a proper logging library.
```

### Block specific MCP tools (regex tool_matcher)

```markdown
---
name: block-slack-mcp
enabled: true
event: all
action: block
tool_matcher: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
conditions:
  - field: tool_name
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "mcp__claude_ai_Slack__.*"
---

Slack MCP tools are blocked in this project.
```

### Require tests before stop

```markdown
---
name: require-tests
enabled: true
event: stop
action: block
conditions:
  - field: transcript
    operator: not_contains
    pattern: "npm test|pytest|cargo test|bun test"
---

No test execution detected. Please run tests before finishing.
```

### Multiple conditions (AND)

```markdown
---
name: warn-env-write
enabled: true
event: file
action: warn
conditions:
  - field: file_path
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "\\.env"
  - field: new_text
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: "(password|secret|token|api_key)\\s*="
---

Writing secrets to .env file. Ensure this file is in .gitignore.
```

### Project override to disable global rule

```markdown
---
name: block-dangerous-rm
enabled: false
event: bash
---

(This project-level rule disables the global block-dangerous-rm rule.)
```

## Tips

- Use `pattern` for simple single-condition rules
- Use `conditions` for multi-field or complex matching
- `tool_matcher` filters BEFORE conditions are checked (performance optimization)
- Test regex patterns with: `echo "test string" | python3 -c "import re,sys; print(bool(re.search(r'pattern', sys.stdin.read())))"`
- Start with `action: warn` and switch to `block` once confident

<commentary>гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢдјҡи©ұеҶ…гҒ®гғҹгӮ№гӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒ—гҒҰгғ•гғғгӮҜеҢ–гҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’жҳҺзӨәзҡ„гҒ«дҫқй јгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ</commentary>
</example>

# дјҡи©ұеҲҶжһҗгӮЁгғјгӮёгӮ§гғігғҲ

дјҡи©ұгҒ®еұҘжӯҙгӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒ—гҖҒhookify гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ§йҳІжӯўгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚ Claude гҒ®е•ҸйЎҢиЎҢеӢ•гӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

## еҲҶжһҗгғ—гғӯгӮ»гӮ№

1. **гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёгӮ’йҖҶжҷӮзі»еҲ—гҒ§гӮ№гӮӯгғЈгғі**гҒ—гҖҒд»ҘдёӢгӮ’жҺўгҒҷ:
   - жҳҺзӨәзҡ„гҒӘдҝ®жӯЈжҢҮзӨә: гҖҢгҖңгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„гҒ§гҖҚгҖҢгҖңгҒҜгӮ„гӮҒгҒҰгҖҚгҖҢгҖңгҒЁиЁҖгҒЈгҒҹгҒ®гҒ«...гҖҚ
   - дёҚжәҖгҒ®гӮ·гӮ°гғҠгғ«: гҖҢгҒӘгҒңгҖңгҒ—гҒҹгҒ®?гҖҚгҖҢй јгӮ“гҒ§гҒ„гҒӘгҒ„гҒ®гҒ«...гҖҚгҖҢгҒҫгҒҹ?!гҖҚ
   - Claude гҒ®гӮўгӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіеҫҢгҒ®жүӢеӢ•гғӘгғҗгғјгғҲгӮ„дҝ®жӯЈ
   - иӨҮж•°гӮҝгғјгғігҒ«гӮҸгҒҹгӮӢз№°гӮҠиҝ”гҒ—гҒ®е•ҸйЎҢ

2. **еҗ„е•ҸйЎҢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰд»ҘдёӢгӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢ:**
   - й–ўйҖЈгҒҷгӮӢгғ„гғјгғ« (Bash, Edit, Write гҒӘгҒ©)
   - иЎҢеӢ•гӮ’гӮӯгғЈгғғгғҒгҒҷгӮӢ regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі
   - йҮҚиҰҒеәҰ: й«ҳ (еҚұйҷә)гҖҒдёӯ (гӮ№гӮҝгӮӨгғ«/иҰҸзҙ„)гҖҒдҪҺ (еҘҪгҒҝ)
   - иӯҰе‘ҠгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚгҒӢгғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚгҒӢ

3. **ж§ӢйҖ еҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹзөҗжһңгӮ’еҮәеҠӣгҒҷгӮӢ:**

```
## е•ҸйЎҢ: [з°ЎжҪ”гҒӘиӘ¬жҳҺ]

- **йҮҚиҰҒеәҰ**: й«ҳ | дёӯ | дҪҺ
- **гғ„гғјгғ«**: [гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі]
- **гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ**: bash | file | stop | prompt | all
- **гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі**: [regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі]
- **гӮўгӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі**: warn | block
- **гӮігғігғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲ**: [дјҡи©ұеҶ…гҒ§дҪ•гҒҢиө·гҒҚгҒҹгҒӢ]

### жҸҗжЎҲгғ«гғјгғ«

---
name: [kebab-case-name]
enabled: true
event: [event]
action: [action]
tool_matcher: [гғ„гғјгғ«гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі (еҝ…иҰҒгҒӘе ҙеҗҲ)]
conditions:
  - field: [field]
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: [pattern]
---

[гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒёгҒ®иӯҰе‘Ҡ/гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮё]
```

## гӮЁгғғгӮёгӮұгғјгӮ№

- д»®иӘ¬зҡ„гҒӘиӯ°и«–гҒҜе•ҸйЎҢгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰжүұгӮҸгҒӘгҒ„
- дёҖеәҰгҒҚгӮҠгҒ®дәӢж•…гҒҜйҮҚиҰҒеәҰгӮ’дҪҺгҒҸиЁӯе®ҡгҒҷгӮӢ
- дё»иҰізҡ„гҒӘеҘҪгҒҝгҒҜиЁҳйҢІгҒҷгӮӢгҒҢе„Әе…ҲеәҰгҒҜдҪҺгҒ„

дјҡи©ұгҒ®еұҘжӯҙгӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒ—гҖҒгғ•гғғгӮҜгҒ§йҳІжӯўгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚиЎҢеӢ•гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгӮЁгғјгӮёгӮ§гғігғҲгҖӮ

<example>
Context: гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢ /hookify гӮігғһгғігғүгӮ’еј•ж•°гҒӘгҒ—гҒ§е®ҹиЎҢгҒ—гҒҹ

<commentary>/hookify гӮ’еј•ж•°гҒӘгҒ—гҒ§е®ҹиЎҢгҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒдјҡи©ұеҲҶжһҗгғўгғјгғүгҒҢгғҲгғӘгӮ¬гғјгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮ</commentary>
</example>

<example>

user: "гҒ“гҒ®дјҡи©ұгӮ’жҢҜгӮҠиҝ”гҒЈгҒҰгҖҒгҒӮгҒӘгҒҹгҒ®гғҹгӮ№гҒ«еҜҫгҒҷгӮӢгғ•гғғгӮҜгӮ’дҪңгҒЈгҒҰ"
assistant: "conversation-analyzer гӮЁгғјгӮёгӮ§гғігғҲгҒ§е•ҸйЎҢгӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒ—гҖҒгғ«гғјгғ«гӮ’жҸҗжЎҲгҒ—гҒҫгҒҷгҖӮ"
<commentary>гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒҢдјҡи©ұеҶ…гҒ®гғҹгӮ№гӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒ—гҒҰгғ•гғғгӮҜеҢ–гҒҷгӮӢгҒ“гҒЁгӮ’жҳҺзӨәзҡ„гҒ«дҫқй јгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮ</commentary>
</example>

# дјҡи©ұеҲҶжһҗгӮЁгғјгӮёгӮ§гғігғҲ

дјҡи©ұгҒ®еұҘжӯҙгӮ’еҲҶжһҗгҒ—гҖҒhookify гғ«гғјгғ«гҒ§йҳІжӯўгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚ Claude гҒ®е•ҸйЎҢиЎҢеӢ•гӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢгҖӮ

## еҲҶжһҗгғ—гғӯгӮ»гӮ№

1. **гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮёгӮ’йҖҶжҷӮзі»еҲ—гҒ§гӮ№гӮӯгғЈгғі**гҒ—гҖҒд»ҘдёӢгӮ’жҺўгҒҷ:
   - жҳҺзӨәзҡ„гҒӘдҝ®жӯЈжҢҮзӨә: гҖҢгҖңгҒ—гҒӘгҒ„гҒ§гҖҚгҖҢгҖңгҒҜгӮ„гӮҒгҒҰгҖҚгҖҢгҖңгҒЁиЁҖгҒЈгҒҹгҒ®гҒ«...гҖҚ
   - дёҚжәҖгҒ®гӮ·гӮ°гғҠгғ«: гҖҢгҒӘгҒңгҖңгҒ—гҒҹгҒ®?гҖҚгҖҢй јгӮ“гҒ§гҒ„гҒӘгҒ„гҒ®гҒ«...гҖҚгҖҢгҒҫгҒҹ?!гҖҚ
   - Claude гҒ®гӮўгӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғіеҫҢгҒ®жүӢеӢ•гғӘгғҗгғјгғҲгӮ„дҝ®жӯЈ
   - иӨҮж•°гӮҝгғјгғігҒ«гӮҸгҒҹгӮӢз№°гӮҠиҝ”гҒ—гҒ®е•ҸйЎҢ

2. **еҗ„е•ҸйЎҢгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰд»ҘдёӢгӮ’зү№е®ҡгҒҷгӮӢ:**
   - й–ўйҖЈгҒҷгӮӢгғ„гғјгғ« (Bash, Edit, Write гҒӘгҒ©)
   - иЎҢеӢ•гӮ’гӮӯгғЈгғғгғҒгҒҷгӮӢ regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі
   - йҮҚиҰҒеәҰ: й«ҳ (еҚұйҷә)гҖҒдёӯ (гӮ№гӮҝгӮӨгғ«/иҰҸзҙ„)гҖҒдҪҺ (еҘҪгҒҝ)
   - иӯҰе‘ҠгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚгҒӢгғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгҒҷгҒ№гҒҚгҒӢ

3. **ж§ӢйҖ еҢ–гҒ•гӮҢгҒҹзөҗжһңгӮ’еҮәеҠӣгҒҷгӮӢ:**

```
## е•ҸйЎҢ: [з°ЎжҪ”гҒӘиӘ¬жҳҺ]

- **йҮҚиҰҒеәҰ**: й«ҳ | дёӯ | дҪҺ
- **гғ„гғјгғ«**: [гғ„гғјгғ«еҗҚгҒҫгҒҹгҒҜгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі]
- **гӮӨгғҷгғігғҲ**: bash | file | stop | prompt | all
- **гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі**: [regex гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі]
- **гӮўгӮҜгӮ·гғ§гғі**: warn | block
- **гӮігғігғҶгӮӯгӮ№гғҲ**: [дјҡи©ұеҶ…гҒ§дҪ•гҒҢиө·гҒҚгҒҹгҒӢ]

### жҸҗжЎҲгғ«гғјгғ«

---
name: [kebab-case-name]
enabled: true
event: [event]
action: [action]
tool_matcher: [гғ„гғјгғ«гғ‘гӮҝгғјгғі (еҝ…иҰҒгҒӘе ҙеҗҲ)]
conditions:
  - field: [field]
    operator: regex_match
    pattern: [pattern]
---

[гғҰгғјгӮ¶гғјгҒёгҒ®иӯҰе‘Ҡ/гғ–гғӯгғғгӮҜгғЎгғғгӮ»гғјгӮё]
```

## гӮЁгғғгӮёгӮұгғјгӮ№

- д»®иӘ¬зҡ„гҒӘиӯ°и«–гҒҜе•ҸйЎҢгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰжүұгӮҸгҒӘгҒ„
- дёҖеәҰгҒҚгӮҠгҒ®дәӢж•…гҒҜйҮҚиҰҒеәҰгӮ’дҪҺгҒҸиЁӯе®ҡгҒҷгӮӢ
- дё»иҰізҡ„гҒӘеҘҪгҒҝгҒҜиЁҳйҢІгҒҷгӮӢгҒҢе„Әе…ҲеәҰгҒҜдҪҺгҒ„
- е•ҸйЎҢгҒҢиҰӢгҒӨгҒӢгӮүгҒӘгҒ„е ҙеҗҲгҒҜгҖҢжӨңеҮәеҸҜиғҪгҒӘгғ‘гӮҝгғјгғігҒҜгҒӮгӮҠгҒҫгҒӣгӮ“гҒ§гҒ—гҒҹгҖҚгҒЁе ұе‘ҠгҒҷгӮӢ
